##### 2.3.3.7 PDSCH rate matching around LTE CRS, LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH and CAT-M

###### 2.3.3.7.1 PDSCH rate matching around LTE channels/signals overview

###### 2.3.3.7.1.0-1 PDSCH rate matching around LTE channels/signals overview general cases (ID: 11098240)

**2.3.3.7.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11098246`)

PDSCH rate matching around LTE channels/signals overview
For FDD and Loner, Loki [CB008325-5G-BA] and Thor [End CB008325-5G-BA]  L1 HWs

For the LTE CRS mapping, there are below options:

 No LTE CRS: The PDSCH is type A mapping without specific configuration.

 PDSCH rate matching around LTE CRS: The PDSCH REs are mapped according to LTE CRS rate matching (see 38.214 §5.1.4.2, 5G\_UP\_ALG\_453\_replaced([11098260](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bEfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 LTE CRS puncture NR PDSCH: The PDSCH REs are punctured by LTE CRS REs (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_477\_replaced([11098701](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3V_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Addtional DMRS shift: The additional DMRS is shift from 11 to 12 (see 3GPP 38.211 §7.4.1.1.2, and also 5G\_UP\_ALG\_501\_replaced([11098945](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knBsgfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), but there is no LTE CRS rate matching or puncturing.

---

###### 2.3.3.7.1.0-2 PDSCH rate matching around LTE channels/signals overview additional DSS cases due to flexible rate matching (PSS/SSS/PBCH) (ID: 11098250)

**2.3.3.7.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11098256`)

PDSCH rate matching around LTE channels/signals overview
For FDD and Loner, Loki [CB008325-5G-BA] and Thor [End CB008325-5G-BA]  L1 HWs

For the LTE CRS mapping, there are below additional options:

 PDSCH rate matching around LTE PBCH, the PDSCH RB are mapped according to LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH rate matching which is applied (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_505\_replaced([11098967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knBsjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) instead of LTE CRS rate matching.

 PDSCH rate matching around LTE PSS (7PRBs), the PDSCH RB are mapped according to LTE PSS/SSS rate matching (7 PRBs) which is applied (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_505\_replaced([11098967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knBsjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) instead of LTE CRS rate matching.

 PDSCH rate matching around LTE PSS (6PRBs), the PDSCH RB are mapped according to LTE PSS/SSS rate matching (6 PRBs) which is applied (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_505\_replaced([11098967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knBsjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) instead of LTE CRS rate matching.

---

###### 2.3.3.7.2 PDSCH RM around LTE CRS

###### 2.3.3.7.2.0-1 PDSCH RM around LTE CRS Overview (ID: 11098262)

**2.3.3.7.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11098265`)

PDSCH RM around LTE CRS
For FDD, the PDSCH RM around LTE CRS, 5G-L1-DL will withdraw some physical resource elements in each RB of the PDSCH granted according to the following parameters (otherwise collision with LTE CRS REs, see lte-CRS-ToMatchAround IE in 3GPP 38.214 §5.1.4.2):

* the number of LTE CRS ports
* the 3GPP parameter cell-specific frequency shift:  LTE-CRS-vshift.
* the LTE carrier bandwidth provided by below table

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| LTE Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | LTE carrier bandwidth (RBs) |
| 20 MHz (FDD) | 100 |
| 15 MHz (FDD) | 75 |
| 10 MHz (FDD) | 50 |

* the position of each RB granted according to the central frequency of the NR carrier bandwidth (the mapping is different due to the fact in LTE the central frequency (that is DC carrier: Direct Current) is bypassed in DL baseband OFDM signal, see 36.211 §6.12).

The position of central frequency of the NR carrier bandwidth (ARFCN frequency (see 38.104) which is identical with the center frequency of LTE bandwidth, see 36.104) depends on downlink bandwidth. This position means the PRB index in the NR carrier bandwidth and the subcarrier index within this PRB as illustrated in the below table:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NR carrier Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | NR carrier bandwidth (RBs) | ARFCN (or Dc) frequency |  |
| central RB | subcarrier of central RB |  |  |
| 20 MHz (FDD) | 106 | 53 | 0 |
| 15 MHz (FDD) | 79 | 39 | 6 |
| 10 MHz (FDD) | 52 | 26 | 0 |

If the additional DMRS is not used, all the REs of the symbol 12 are used for PDSCH data REs.

If the number of DMRS CDM group without data is equal to 2, the REs in the same symbol than DMRS (front Load or additional) and not used by DMRS cannot be used for PDSCH data. If it is set to 1, these REs are used as PDSCH data REs (38.214 §5.1.6.2).

Note: for FDD DSS, PTRS is disabled for PDSCH with LTE CRS rate matching.

In practice, the number of LTE CRS REs is computed by 5G-L2-PS and is provided to 5G-L1-DL for each PDSCH granted. For the different number of LTE CRS ports,  the LTE CRS mapping are different, refer to 36.211 section 6.12 for detailed RE mappings.

---

###### 2.3.3.7.2.0-2 PDSCH RM around LTE CRS Support PDSCH RM around LTE CRS in L1 HW (ID: 11098269)

**2.3.3.7.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11098276`)

PDSCH RM around LTE CRS
The PDSCH RM around LTE CRS is supported for FDD and L1 HW as per table below:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| L1 HW | PDSCH RM around LTE CRS 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14805\_replaced([11098262](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bEvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Loner | supported |
| Loki | supported |
| Thor | [CB008325-5G-BA] and Thor [End CB008325-5G-BA] |

.

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 8 rows and 16 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of 8 light gray squares. Next to the gray squares is a column of 8 black squares, alternating with 4 blue squares. The remaining 12 columns are filled with light green squares, with some red squares interspersed. The rightmost column consists of 8 black squares, alternating with 4 purple squares.
The red squares are located at the following positions (row, column):
(2, 5), (2, 8)
(3, 5), (3, 8)
(4, 5), (4, 8)
(5, 5), (5, 8)
(6, 5), (6, 8)
The purple squares are located at the following positions (row, column):
(2, 15)
(4, 15)
(6, 15)
(8, 15)
The blue squares are located at the following positions (row, column):
(2, 4)
(4, 4)
(6, 4)
(8, 4)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_949.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 8 rows and 13 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares. The second column from the left has 8 light gray squares. The third column from the left has 8 blue squares. The remaining 10 columns are filled with light green squares, with some red and purple squares interspersed. Specifically, there are red squares in the following positions (row, column, counting from top-left as 1,1): (1,5), (1,8), (2,4), (2,7), (3,5), (3,8), (4,4), (4,7), (5,5), (5,8), (6,4), (6,7), (7,5), (7,8), (8,4), (8,7). There are purple squares in the following positions: (1,11), (3,11), (5,11), (7,11), (8,11). There are black squares in the following positions: (1,3), (2,3), (3,3), (4,3), (5,3), (6,3), (7,3), (8,3), (1,10), (2,10), (4,10), (6,10), (7,10).[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_949.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 8 rows and 13 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares. The second column from the left contains 8 gray squares. The third column from the left has 8 black squares. The fourth column from the left contains 8 blue squares. The remaining 9 columns are filled with light green squares, with some red squares interspersed. Specifically, in the light green section, red squares appear in the following positions (row, column, counting from the top left of the entire grid): (1, 5), (1, 9), (2, 5), (2, 10), (3, 5), (3, 11), (4, 5), (4, 12), (5, 5), (5, 13), (6, 5), (6, 10), (7, 5), (7, 11), (8, 5), (8, 12). The rightmost two columns of the grid are black and purple. The 12th column from the left has 8 black squares, and the 13th column from the left has 8 purple squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_949.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which extends to the right and downwards, is filled with a repeating pattern of light green, black, and purple squares. Specifically, there are five columns of these squares.
The first of these five columns contains alternating light green and black squares, with a light green square at the top and a black square at the bottom.
The second column consists of alternating light green and purple squares, with a light green square at the top and a purple square at the bottom.
The third column is identical to the first, with alternating light green and black squares.
The fourth column is identical to the second, with alternating light green and purple squares.
The fifth column is also identical to the first and third, with alternating light green and black squares.
Within these five columns, there are also scattered red squares. These red squares appear in specific positions within the rows. For example, in the first row of the patterned section, there is a red square in the third column. In the second row, there is a red square in the first column. In the third row, there is a red square in the third column. In the fourth row, there is a red square in the first column. In the fifth row, there is a red square in the third column. In the sixth row, there is a red square in the first column. In the seventh row, there is a red square in the third column. In the eighth row, there is a red square in the first column.
The overall impression is a structured, colorful grid with a clear visual pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_951.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns. On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares. The remaining part of the grid, which extends to the right and downwards, is filled with a repeating pattern of light green, black, red, and purple squares. Specifically, the pattern appears to be: light green, black, red, light green, purple, light green, black, red, light green, purple, and so on, repeating across the columns. The rows also exhibit a pattern, with variations in the placement of the red and purple squares within the light green and black background. The grid appears to be approximately 8 rows high and 12 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_951.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 8 rows and 10 columns of squares.
The first column of squares is entirely red.
The second column consists of gray squares.
The third column contains alternating blue and black squares, starting with blue at the top.
The fourth column is entirely light green.
The fifth column has alternating black and red squares, starting with black at the top.
The sixth column is entirely light green.
The seventh column has alternating purple and black squares, starting with purple at the top.
The eighth column is entirely light green.
The ninth column has alternating red and black squares, starting with red at the top.
The tenth column is entirely light green.
The pattern of colors within the grid suggests a structured arrangement, possibly representing data or a visual pattern. There is no discernible text within the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_951.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a vertical strip of 8 red squares, followed by a strip of 8 light gray squares, and then a strip of 8 blue squares.
The main body of the grid consists of 8 rows and 12 columns of light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several red squares scattered throughout. Specifically, red squares appear in the following positions (row, column), counting from the top-left of the light green area:
- Row 1: Column 3, Column 7
- Row 2: Column 2, Column 6
- Row 3: Column 4, Column 8
- Row 4: Column 2, Column 6
- Row 5: Column 3, Column 7
- Row 6: Column 2, Column 6
- Row 7: Column 4, Column 8
- Row 8: Column 2, Column 6
To the right of the light green area, there is a vertical strip of 8 squares. This strip alternates between black and purple squares, starting with black at the top and ending with purple at the bottom.
The image does not contain any discernible text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_953.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]doors://esdooapv51.emea.nsn-net.net:36655/?version=2&prodID=0&view=00000036&urn=urn:telelogic::1-4017b9fe29051a61-O-467-00025ecc

Scattered within the green squares are red squares. On the right side of the grid, there are two columns of colored squares: black and purple.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide.
The first column from the left is entirely red.
The second column is entirely gray.
The third column consists of blue squares.
The fourth column is entirely black.
The fifth through eleventh columns are predominantly light green, with some red squares interspersed.
The twelfth column is entirely purple.

On the left side of the grid, there are three columns of solid colors: red, gray, and black. The red column consists of eight squares. The gray column has eight squares, and the black column has eight squares.
To the right of these initial columns, there is a larger section of the grid filled with light green squares. Within this light green section, there are scattered red squares.
Further to the right, there are two more columns of solid colors: black and purple. The black column has eight squares, and the purple column has eight squares.

   [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares.
To the right of the red squares is a column of seven gray squares.
The next column contains five blue squares interspersed with black squares. Specifically, there are blue squares in the 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, and 10th rows from the top.
The remaining columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, black, red, and purple squares.
The grid appears to be 10 rows high and 10 columns wide, although the exact dimensions are not perfectly clear due to the black background extending beyond the grid.
The pattern of colors in the main grid area (from the 4th column onwards) is as follows:
- Column 4: Alternating light green and black squares, with a red square in the 2nd row and a purple square in the 5th row.
- Column 5: Alternating light green and purple squares, with a red square in the 3rd row.
- Column 6: Alternating light green and black squares, with a red square in the 4th row and a purple square in the 7th row.
- Column 7: Alternating light green and purple squares, with a red square in the 5th row.
- Column 8: Alternating light green and black squares, with a red square in the 6th row and a purple square in the 9th row.
- Column 9: Alternating light green and purple squares, with a red square in the 7th row.
- Column 10: Alternating light green and black squares, with a red square in the 8th row.
The image does not contain any discernible text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_955.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of this, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid is composed of light green squares, interspersed with squares of red, purple, and black.
Let's break down the pattern from the fourth column onwards:
Column 4 (starting from the left, after the blue column):
- Red, Black, Red, Black, Red, Black, Red, Black
Column 5:
- Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple
Column 6:
- Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red
Column 7:
- Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple
Column 8:
- Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red
Column 9:
- Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple, Purple
Column 10:
- Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red, Red
The overall impression is a patterned arrangement of colors within a grid.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_955.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares.

The third column from the left has 10 squares, alternating between blue and black. The pattern is blue, black, blue, black, blue, black, blue, black, blue, black.
The remaining seven columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, red, purple, and black squares.
Specifically, starting from the fourth column:

On the left side of the grid, there are three vertical columns of colored squares. The leftmost column consists of red squares. The second column from the left is composed of light gray squares. The third column contains alternating black and blue squares, with blue squares appearing in rows 2, 4, and 6.
The main body of the grid is filled with light green squares. Scattered within this green area are red squares. These red squares are located in the following positions (row, column, counting from the top-left as 1,1):
- Row 1, Column 4
- Row 1, Column 7
- Row 2, Column 4
- Row 2, Column 7
- Row 3, Column 5

- Row 5, Column 5

- Row 6, Column 4
- Row 6, Column 7

The grid appears to be 7 rows high and 12 columns wide.
The table representation of the colored squares, where 'R' denotes red, 'G' denotes light gray, 'B' denotes blue, 'K' denotes black, 'L' denotes light green, and 'P' denotes purple:

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns. On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there are eight columns of squares, each containing a repeating pattern of colors.
The third column from the left has a single blue square at the top, followed by a black square, then a red square, then a black square, then a blue square, then a black square, then a red square, and finally a black square.
The fourth column from the left has a red square at the top, followed by a light green square, then a light green square, then a red square, then a light green square, then a light green square, then a red square, and finally a light green square.
The fifth column from the left has a light green square at the top, followed by a purple square, then a light green square, then a black square, then a light green square, then a purple square, then a light green square, and finally a black square.
The sixth column from the left has a light green square at the top, followed by a light green square, then a red square, then a light green square, then a light green square, then a red square, then a light green square, and finally a light green square.
The seventh column from the left has a black square at the top, followed by a light green square, then a purple square, then a light green square, then a black square, then a light green square, then a purple square, and finally a light green square.
The eighth column from the left has a light green square at the top, followed by a light green square, then a light green square, then a red square, then a light green square, then a light green square, then a light green square, and finally a red square.
The ninth column from the left has a light green square at the top, followed by a purple square, then a light green square, then a black square, then a light green square, then a purple square, then a light green square, and finally a black square.
The tenth column from the left has a red square at the top, followed by a light green square, then a light green square, then a red square, then a light green square, then a light green square, then a red square, and finally a light green square.
The eleventh column from the left has a light green square at the top, followed by a light green square, then a purple square, then a light green square, then a light green square, then a purple square, then a light green square, and finally a light green square.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_959.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except the central RB )
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns. On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Next to the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares. Following the blue squares, there are alternating columns of black and light green squares. Within the light green squares, there are scattered red and purple squares. Specifically, the pattern of colored squares is as follows:
Column 1: 8 red squares
Column 2: 8 gray squares
Column 3: 8 blue squares
Column 4: 8 black squares
Column 5: 8 light green squares
Column 6: 8 black squares
Column 7: 8 light green squares with red squares at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
Column 8: 8 black squares
Column 9: 8 light green squares with purple squares at rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
Column 10: 8 black squares
Column 11: 8 light green squares with red squares at rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
Column 12: 8 black squares
Column 13: 8 light green squares with purple squares at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
Column 14: 8 black squares
Column 15: 8 light green squares with red squares at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
The image does not contain any discernible text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_959.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 [image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares.
To the right of the red column is a column of 10 gray squares.
Next to the gray column is a column with 10 squares, alternating between blue and black. Specifically, it starts with blue, then black, then blue, then black, and so on, down to the last square which is blue.
The remaining part of the grid is composed of 8 columns, each containing 10 squares. These columns are predominantly light green. Within these light green columns, there are scattered red, purple, and black squares.

- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Purple
- Row 9: Green
- Row 10: Purple
The overall impression is a patterned arrangement of colors within a grid.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_959.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
 
4) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 [image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares.
To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares.
The next column contains eight blue squares.
Following the blue squares is a column of eight black squares.
The fifth column from the left has eight light green squares, with a red square in the second row, a red square in the fourth row, and a red square in the sixth row.
The sixth column from the left has eight light green squares, with a purple square in the second row, a purple square in the fourth row, and a purple square in the sixth row.
The seventh column from the left has eight light green squares, with a black square in the second row, a black square in the fourth row, and a black square in the sixth row.
The eighth column from the left has eight light green squares, with a red square in the second row, a red square in the fourth row, and a red square in the sixth row.
The ninth column from the left has eight light green squares, with a purple square in the second row, a purple square in the fourth row, and a purple square in the sixth row.
The tenth column from the left has eight light green squares, with a black square in the second row, a black square in the fourth row, and a black square in the sixth row.
The eleventh column from the left has eight light green squares, with a red square in the second row, a red square in the fourth row, and a red square in the sixth row.
The twelfth column from the left has eight light green squares, with a purple square in the second row, a purple square in the fourth row, and a purple square in the sixth row.
The grid appears to be 12 columns wide and 8 rows tall. The colors are arranged in a repeating pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_959.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
 
5) for RB which is just above the LTE carrier bandwidth (RB number = number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth - (deltaRbNrLte >> 1) )
    [image-4][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns.
The first column from the left consists of 10 red squares.
The second column contains 10 gray squares.
The third column has 5 blue squares at the top, followed by 5 black squares.
The fourth column is entirely filled with light green squares.
The fifth column has 5 black squares at the top, followed by 5 light green squares.
The sixth column contains 5 purple squares at the top, followed by 5 black squares.
The seventh column is entirely filled with light green squares.
The eighth column has 5 black squares at the top, followed by 5 light green squares.
The ninth column contains 5 purple squares at the top, followed by 5 black squares.
The tenth column is entirely filled with light green squares.
At the bottom of the grid, there are some exceptions to the pattern:
In the last row, the third square from the left is blue, the fourth is red, the fifth is light green, the sixth is purple, and the tenth is red.
In the second to last row, the fourth square from the left is red, and the sixth is purple.
In the third to last row, the fourth square from the left is red, and the sixth is purple.
In the fourth to last row, the fourth square from the left is red, and the sixth is purple.
In the fifth to last row, the fourth square from the left is red, and the sixth is purple.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_959.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares, resembling a pixel art or a simplified game board. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there are several columns of solid red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of light gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of blue squares.
The majority of the grid is filled with light green squares. Within this green expanse, there are scattered red squares, indicating specific marked cells.
On the right side of the grid, there is a column of black squares, and to the right of that, a column of purple squares.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 14 columns wide, with the leftmost 3 columns being red, gray, and blue respectively, followed by 8 columns of green with red markings, and finally 2 columns of black and purple.
There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_962.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 8 rows and 13 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares. The second column from the left is composed of 8 light gray squares. The third column from the left contains 8 black squares, alternating with 8 blue squares. The remaining columns are filled with light green squares, with some red and purple squares interspersed. Specifically, in the fourth column, there are 8 light green squares. In the fifth column, there are 8 light green squares, with red squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8. The sixth column has 8 light green squares. The seventh column has 8 light green squares, with red squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7. The eighth column has 8 light green squares. The ninth column has 8 light green squares, with red squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8. The tenth column has 8 light green squares. The eleventh column has 8 light green squares, with red squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7. The twelfth column consists of 8 black squares, alternating with 8 purple squares. The thirteenth column has 8 light green squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_962.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored cells. The grid has 8 rows and 16 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red cells. The second column from the left has 8 gray cells. The third column from the left has 8 black cells, alternating with 4 blue cells, with the blue cells appearing in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8. The remaining 13 columns are filled with a mix of green and red cells. The majority of these cells are green, indicating a background color. Red cells are scattered throughout these columns, appearing in specific patterns. The rightmost column has 8 black cells, alternating with 4 purple cells, with the purple cells appearing in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.

On the far left, there is a vertical column of red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of light gray squares. Following this is a column of black squares, interspersed with blue squares.
The main body of the grid is filled with light green squares. Within this green area, there are scattered red squares, forming patterns in some rows.
On the far right, there is another vertical column of black squares, interspersed with purple squares.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and approximately 15 columns wide, excluding the colored border columns.
The colors present are red, light gray, black, blue, light green, and purple. The arrangement of colors suggests a structured design, possibly representing data, a game state, or a visual pattern. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_966.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares.
The remaining part of the grid consists of multiple columns of colored squares. Starting from the third column from the left, the pattern of colors appears to repeat in a somewhat staggered fashion.
Let's break down the grid by columns, starting from the third column:
\* **Column 3:** Contains alternating blue and black squares, with a blue square in the first row, a black square in the second, a blue square in the third, and so on, down to the eighth row.
\* **Column 4:** Contains alternating red and light green squares, with a red square in the first row, a light green square in the second, a red square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 5:** Contains alternating light green and black squares, with a light green square in the first row, a black square in the second, a light green square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 6:** Contains alternating purple and light green squares, with a purple square in the first row, a light green square in the second, a purple square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 7:** Contains alternating light green and black squares, with a light green square in the first row, a black square in the second, a light green square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 8:** Contains alternating red and light green squares, with a red square in the first row, a light green square in the second, a red square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 9:** Contains alternating light green and black squares, with a light green square in the first row, a black square in the second, a light green square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 10:** Contains alternating purple and light green squares, with a purple square in the first row, a light green square in the second, a purple square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 11:** Contains alternating light green and black squares, with a light green square in the first row, a black square in the second, a light green square in the third, and so on.
\* **Column 12:** Contains alternating red and light green squares, with a red square in the first row, a light green square in the second, a red square in the third, and so on.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide, with the first two columns being solid red and gray respectively.
Here is a representation of the grid in markdown format, where 'R' is red, 'G' is gray, 'B' is blue, 'Rd' is red, 'LG' is light green, 'Bk' is black, and 'P' is purple.

| There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_968.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 8 rows and 13 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares. The second column from the left has 8 light gray squares. The third column from the left has 8 black squares, with the exception of the 3rd, 5th, and 7th squares from the top, which are blue. The remaining 10 columns are filled with light green squares, interspersed with red squares in specific positions. The second to last column from the right has 8 black squares, with the exception of the 2nd, 4th, 6th, and 8th squares from the top, which are purple. The rightmost column consists of 8 light green squares.
The red squares are located in the following positions (row, column), counting from the top-left as (1,1):
(1,5), (1,7), (1,10)
(2,5), (2,7)
(3,5), (3,7)
(4,5), (4,7)
(5,5), (5,7)
(6,5), (6,7)
(7,5), (7,7)
(8,5), (8,7)
The blue squares are located in the 3rd column from the left at rows 3, 5, and 7.
The purple squares are located in the second to last column from the right at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_970.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares, resembling a pixel art or a simplified game board. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there are several columns of solid red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of light gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of blue squares.
The majority of the grid is filled with light green squares. Within this green expanse, there are scattered red squares, indicating specific marked cells.
On the right side of the grid, there is a column of black squares, and to the right of that, a column of purple squares.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 14 columns wide, with the leftmost 3 columns being red, gray, and blue respectively, followed by 8 columns of green with red markings, and finally 2 columns of black and purple.
There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_970.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 8 rows and 16 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares. The second column from the left contains 8 gray squares. The third column from the left has 8 black squares, alternating with blue squares. Specifically, the first, third, fifth, and seventh squares in this column are black, while the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth squares are blue.
The remaining 13 columns are filled with a light green background. Within this green area, there are several red squares scattered throughout.
Here's a breakdown of the red squares' positions (row, column, starting from the top-left as 1,1):
- Row 1: Column 6, Column 8, Column 13
- Row 2: Column 5, Column 7, Column 13
- Row 3: Column 5, Column 7, Column 13
- Row 4: Column 5, Column 7, Column 13
- Row 5: Column 5, Column 7, Column 13
- Row 6: Column 5, Column 7, Column 13
- Row 7: Column 5, Column 7, Column 13
- Row 8: Column 5, Column 7, Column 13
The rightmost two columns of the grid contain black and purple squares. The 14th column from the left has 8 black squares. The 15th column from the left has 8 purple squares. The 16th column from the left has 8 black squares.
The image is a visual representation, possibly a heatmap or a matrix, with distinct color coding. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_970.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
 
4) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 10 rows and 15 columns. The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of 10 light gray squares. Next to the gray squares is a column of 10 black squares, each alternating with a blue square. The remaining 12 columns are filled with light green squares, with some red and purple squares interspersed. Specifically, there are red squares in the following positions (row, column): (1, 13), (3, 13), (5, 13), (7, 13), (9, 13), (1, 15), (3, 15), (5, 15), (7, 15), (9, 15). There are purple squares in the following positions (row, column): (1, 16), (3, 16), (5, 16), (7, 16), (9, 16). The grid is bordered by black on the left and bottom.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_970.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
 
5) for RB which is just above the LTE carrier bandwidth (RB number =  number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth - (deltaRbNrLte >> 1) )
 
[image-4][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid is organized into rows and columns, with black lines delineating each square.
On the left side of the grid, there are several columns of solid colors. The first column consists of red squares. The second column contains light gray squares. The third column is made up of blue squares.
The majority of the grid is filled with light green squares.
Towards the right side of the grid, there is a column of black squares interspersed with purple squares. Specifically, there is a black square at the top, followed by a purple square, then a black square, another purple square, and so on, alternating between black and purple squares down the column.
At the bottom of the grid, there are a few red squares scattered within the light green area. There are two red squares in the second to last column from the left, and two more red squares in the fourth column from the right.
The overall impression is a patterned grid with distinct color blocks and some scattered elements.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_970.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
   [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares.
To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares.
Next to the gray squares is a column of eight blue squares.
Following the blue squares is a column of eight black squares.
The remaining columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, red, and purple squares. Specifically, the fifth column from the left has light green squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows, and red squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
The sixth column from the left has purple squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows, and black squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
The seventh column from the left has light green squares in all eight rows.
The eighth column from the left has red squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows, and light green squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
The ninth column from the left has purple squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows, and black squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
The tenth column from the left has light green squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows, and red squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
The eleventh column from the left has red squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows, and light green squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 11 columns wide.
There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_972.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]  
 
 2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares.
To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares.
Following the gray squares is a column of five blue squares, each separated by a black square.
The remaining columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, red, and purple squares, interspersed with black squares.
Specifically, the pattern appears to be:
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating red and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating red and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
The grid has 8 rows and 10 columns of colored squares. The first two columns are solid red and gray respectively. The third column has blue squares in the first five rows, with black squares in the remaining three rows. The subsequent columns exhibit the repeating pattern described above.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_972.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
  [image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares stacked vertically. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there are alternating columns of black and blue squares. The blue squares appear in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows of this section.
The main body of the grid is composed of light green squares. Within this green expanse, there are scattered red, purple, and black squares. The red squares are located in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows, appearing in the third, fifth, and seventh columns from the left edge of the green section. Purple squares are found in the first, third, fifth, and seventh columns of the green section, and they are present in every row. Black squares are interspersed between the purple squares, also appearing in every row.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide, with the first two columns being solid red and gray respectively. The subsequent columns are a mix of colors as described.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_972.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
4) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares.
To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares.
The next column contains eight blue squares.
Following the blue squares is a column of eight black squares.
The fifth column from the left is filled with eight light green squares.
The sixth column from the left contains eight purple squares.
The seventh column from the left is filled with eight light green squares.
The eighth column from the left contains eight red squares.
The ninth column from the left is filled with eight light green squares.
The tenth column from the left contains eight purple squares.
The eleventh column from the left is filled with eight light green squares.
The twelfth column from the left contains eight red squares.
The grid appears to be 12 columns wide and 8 rows tall. The colors are arranged in a repeating pattern within the grid.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_972.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
5) for RB which is just above the LTE carrier bandwidth (RB number =  number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth - (deltaRbNrLte >> 1) )
   [image-4][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 8 rows and 10 columns.
The first column consists of 8 red squares.
The second column contains 8 gray squares.
The third column has 5 blue squares in the top portion, followed by 3 black squares at the bottom.
The fourth column is entirely filled with light green squares.
The fifth column has 5 black squares in the top portion, followed by 3 purple squares at the bottom.
The sixth column is entirely filled with light green squares.
The seventh column has 5 black squares in the top portion, followed by 3 purple squares at the bottom.
The eighth column is entirely filled with light green squares.
The ninth column has 5 black squares in the top portion, followed by 3 red squares at the bottom.
The tenth column is entirely filled with light green squares.
The colors are arranged in a repeating pattern of red, gray, blue, green, black, green, black, green, black, green. However, the pattern is disrupted in the lower rows, where purple and red squares appear.
Specifically, the pattern of colors in each row from left to right is:
Row 1: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green
Row 2: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green
Row 3: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green
Row 4: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green
Row 5: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green
Row 6: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Purple, Green, Red, Green
Row 7: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Purple, Green, Red, Green
Row 8: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Purple, Green, Red, Green[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_972.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

###### 2.3.3.7.2.1.3 One LTE CRS Port

###### 2.3.3.7.2.1.3-1 One LTE CRS Port

**2.3.3.7.2.1.3-1.0-3**  (ID: `11098690`)

One LTE CRS Port
For each L1 HW and for FDD, the different configurations where the LTE CRS mapping needs to be applied are:

* function of the value of the number of LTE CRS ports and the cell-specific frequency shift,
* depicted with legends details provided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_461\_replaced([11098281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),
* supported as in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | no. of LTE CRS port = 2 5G\_UP\_ALG\_463\_replaced([11098299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | no. of LTE CRS port = 4 5G\_UP\_ALG\_470\_replaced([11098393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km-CK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | no. of LTE CRS port = 1 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12861\_replaced([11098488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km-pPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Loner | supported | supported | - |
| Loki | supported | supported | supported |
| Thor | supported | supported | [CB009029-5G-A] supported [End CB009029-5G-A] |

---

###### 2.3.3.7.2.1.3-2 One LTE CRS Port

**2.3.3.7.2.1.3-2.0-1**  (ID: `11098697`)

One LTE CRS Port
For each L1 HW and for FDD, the different configurations where the LTE CRS mapping needs to be applied with Two Additional DMRS are:

* function of the value of the number of LTE CRS ports and the cell-specific frequency shift,
* depicted with legends details provided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23700\_replaced([11098290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),
* supported as in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | no. of LTE CRS port = 2 5G\_UP\_ALG\_463\_replaced([11098299](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bIPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | no. of LTE CRS port = 4 5G\_UP\_ALG\_470\_replaced([11098393](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km-CK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | no. of LTE CRS port = 1 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12861\_replaced([11098488](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km-pPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Loner | supported | supported | - |
| Loki | supported | supported | supported |
| Thor | supported | supported | supported |

For FDD
 
Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_461\_replaced([11098281](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored cells. The grid has 10 rows and 15 columns. The leftmost column consists of 10 red cells. To the right of the red cells is a column of 10 gray cells. Next to the gray cells is a column of 10 black cells, each separated by a blue cell. The remaining 12 columns are predominantly filled with light green cells. Within these green cells, there are several red cells scattered throughout. Specifically, red cells appear in the following positions (row, column, counting from top-left as 1,1): (2, 6), (4, 10), (5, 7), (7, 5), (8, 12), and (10, 7). The rightmost column of the grid contains 10 black cells, each separated by a purple cell. The background of the image is black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_975.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 10 rows and 15 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares followed by 2 gray squares. The second column from the left has 8 gray squares followed by 2 blue squares. The third column from the left has 10 black squares. The remaining 12 columns are predominantly light green, with some red and purple squares interspersed.
Specifically, the red squares are located at:
- Row 1, Column 5
- Row 3, Column 7
- Row 5, Column 5
- Row 5, Column 13
- Row 7, Column 5
- Row 9, Column 5
The purple squares are located at:
- Row 2, Column 14
- Row 4, Column 14
- Row 6, Column 14
- Row 8, Column 14
The grid appears to be a visual representation of data, possibly a heatmap or a game board. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_975.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
 
 3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid is organized into rows and columns, with black lines separating each square.
On the left side of the grid, there are three vertical columns of colored squares. The leftmost column consists of red squares. The middle column contains light gray squares. The third column from the left is composed of blue squares.
The majority of the grid is filled with light green squares. Scattered within this green area are several red squares.
On the right side of the grid, there are two vertical columns of colored squares. The first column from the right is black. The rightmost column consists of purple squares.
The grid appears to be a visual representation, possibly a game board or a data visualization. There is no discernible text in the image.
The table representation of the grid is as follows:

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares stacked vertically.
To the right of the red squares is a column of seven gray squares.
The next column contains a sequence of blue squares interspersed with black squares. Specifically, there are three blue squares, followed by a black square, then another blue square, a black square, and finally a blue square.
The subsequent columns are predominantly filled with light green squares. Within these green sections, there are scattered red, purple, and black squares.
Let's break down the grid by columns, starting from the column after the gray squares:
Column 4 (first column with green):
- Row 1: Red
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Green
- Row 5: Red
- Row 6: Green
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Green
Column 5:
- Row 1: Black
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Green
- Row 5: Black
- Row 6: Green
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Black
Column 6:
- Row 1: Purple
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Purple
- Row 5: Green
- Row 6: Purple
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Purple
Column 7:
- Row 1: Green
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Green
- Row 5: Red
- Row 6: Green
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Green
Column 8:
- Row 1: Black
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Black
- Row 5: Green
- Row 6: Black
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Black
Column 9:
- Row 1: Purple
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Purple
- Row 5: Green
- Row 6: Purple
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Purple
Column 10:
- Row 1: Green
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Green
- Row 5: Green
- Row 6: Green
- Row 7: Red
- Row 8: Green
Column 11:
- Row 1: Black
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Black
- Row 5: Green
- Row 6: Black
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Black
Column 12:
- Row 1: Green
- Row 2: Green
- Row 3: Green
- Row 4: Green
- Row 5: Green
- Row 6: Green
- Row 7: Green
- Row 8: Red
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide, with the first two columns being solid red and gray respectively. The remaining columns are a mix of colors as described above.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_977.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns.
The first column from the left consists of 10 red squares.
The second column contains 10 gray squares.
The third column has 10 blue squares.
The remaining seven columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, black, and purple squares. Specifically, the fourth column is entirely light green. The fifth column alternates between black and light green squares, starting with black at the top. The sixth column is entirely purple. The seventh column alternates between black and light green squares, starting with black at the top. The eighth column is entirely purple. The ninth column alternates between black and light green squares, starting with black at the top. The tenth column contains a mix of light green and red squares. The red squares are located in rows 1, 4, 7, and 10.
The overall impression is a structured, abstract pattern created by the arrangement of these colored squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_977.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns.
The first column from the left consists entirely of red squares.
The second column contains gray squares.
The third column has alternating blue and black squares, with blue squares appearing in rows 2, 3, 4, and 6, and black squares in rows 1, 5, 7, 8, 9, and 10.
The remaining columns (4 through 10) are predominantly filled with light green squares. However, there are several interspersed colored squares within these columns:
- **Column 4:** Contains a red square in row 2 and a red square in row 6.
- **Column 5:** Contains purple squares in rows 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, and 10.
- **Column 6:** Contains black squares in rows 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, and 10, and a purple square in row 7.
- **Column 7:** Contains purple squares in rows 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, and 10.
- **Column 8:** Contains a red square in row 1 and a red square in row 9.
- **Column 9:** Contains purple squares in rows 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, and 10.
- **Column 10:** Contains a red square in row 1 and a red square in row 9.
The overall impression is a patterned grid with distinct color blocks and interspersed accent colors.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_977.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 10 rows and 15 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares followed by 2 gray squares. The second column from the left has 8 gray squares followed by 2 blue squares. The third column from the left has 10 black squares. The remaining 12 columns are predominantly light green, with some red and purple squares interspersed.
Specifically, the red squares are located at:
- Row 1, Column 5
- Row 3, Column 7
- Row 5, Column 5
- Row 5, Column 13
- Row 7, Column 5
- Row 9, Column 5
The purple squares are located at:
- Row 2, Column 14
- Row 4, Column 14
- Row 6, Column 14
- Row 8, Column 14
The grid appears to be a visual representation of data, possibly a heatmap or a game board. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_979.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 12 columns. The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares. The second column from the left is made up of 10 light gray squares. The third column contains 10 black squares. The fourth column is filled with 10 blue squares. The remaining 8 columns are filled with light green squares, with a few exceptions. In the fifth column, there are red squares in rows 1, 4, and 7. In the seventh column, there is a red square in row 2. In the ninth column, there is a red square in row 5. In the eleventh column, there are red squares in rows 3 and 8. The twelfth column consists of alternating black and purple squares, starting with black at the top. Specifically, the squares in rows 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9 are black, while the squares in rows 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10 are purple.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_979.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 10 rows and 16 columns. The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares. The second column from the left contains 10 light gray squares. The third column from the left has 10 black squares. The fourth column from the left contains 10 blue squares. The remaining 12 columns are filled with light green squares, with some exceptions.
Specifically, within the light green area:
- Row 1, Column 5 has a red square.
- Row 2, Column 10 has a red square.
- Row 3, Column 7 has a red square.
- Row 4, Column 13 has a red square.
- Row 5, Column 11 has a red square.
- Row 6, Column 15 has a red square.
- Row 7, Column 12 has a red square.
- Row 8, Column 14 has a red square.
- Row 9, Column 16 has a red square.
- Row 10, Column 15 has a red square.
The rightmost two columns of the grid are also distinct. The second column from the right consists of 10 black squares. The rightmost column contains 10 purple squares.
The image is presented on a black background. There is no visible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_979.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
    [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid is organized into rows and columns. On the far left, there is a column of red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of blue squares. The rest of the grid is filled with light green squares, interspersed with black, purple, and red squares.
Specifically, the grid appears to be 10 columns wide and 10 rows tall.
Column 1: All red squares.
Column 2: All gray squares.
Column 3: Blue squares in rows 3, 5, 7, and 9. Black squares in rows 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10.
Column 4: Red squares in rows 2, 5, and 9. Light green squares in rows 1, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, and 10.
Column 5: Purple squares in rows 3, 5, 7, and 9. Black squares in rows 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10.
Column 6: Light green squares in rows 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10.
Column 7: Red squares in rows 1, 4, and 8. Purple squares in rows 2, 6, and 10. Black squares in rows 3, 5, 7, and 9.
Column 8: Light green squares in rows 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10.
Column 9: Purple squares in rows 3, 5, 7, and 9. Black squares in rows 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10.
Column 10: Red squares in rows 1 and 8. Light green squares in rows 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, and 10.
The image is a visual representation of a grid pattern with distinct color blocks.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_981.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
   [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns.
The first column from the left consists of 10 red squares.
The second column contains 10 gray squares.
The third column has 10 blue squares.
The fourth column is entirely black.
The fifth column is entirely light green.
The sixth column is entirely black.
The seventh column is entirely light green.
The eighth column is entirely black.
The ninth column is entirely light green.
The tenth column is entirely light green.
Within the light green sections, there are scattered red and purple squares.
Specifically, in the fifth column (light green):
- Row 1: Red
- Row 4: Red
- Row 7: Red
In the seventh column (light green):
- Row 2: Purple
- Row 5: Purple
- Row 8: Purple
In the ninth column (light green):
- Row 3: Purple
- Row 6: Purple
- Row 9: Purple
In the tenth column (light green):
- Row 1: Red
- Row 4: Red
- Row 7: Red[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_981.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
     [image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns of squares.
The first column of squares is entirely red.
The second column consists of gray squares.
The third column has blue squares interspersed with black squares. Specifically, from top to bottom, the pattern is blue, blue, blue, black, blue, black, blue, black, blue, black.
The remaining columns are predominantly light green, with some squares colored red, purple, and black.
Let's break down the grid by rows, starting from the top:
Row 1: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Red
Row 2: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green
Row 3: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 4: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green
Row 5: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Red, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 6: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Purple, Green
Row 7: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Black, Green
Row 8: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Purple, Green
Row 9: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Red, Green, Black, Green, Black, Red
Row 10: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Purple, Green, Purple, Green
The image appears to be a visual representation of data or a pattern, possibly a heatmap or a game board. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_981.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 12 columns. The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares. The second column from the left is made up of 10 light gray squares. The third column contains 10 black squares. The fourth column is filled with 10 blue squares. The remaining 8 columns are filled with light green squares, with a few exceptions. In the fifth column, there are red squares in rows 1, 4, and 7. In the seventh column, there is a red square in row 2. In the ninth column, there is a red square in row 5. In the eleventh column, there are red squares in rows 3 and 8. The twelfth column consists of alternating black and purple squares, starting with black at the top. Specifically, the squares in rows 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9 are black, while the squares in rows 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10 are purple.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_983.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
 
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid is organized into rows and columns. On the left side of the grid, there are vertical columns of red, gray, and blue squares. These columns are followed by a wider section of light green squares, which contains several red squares scattered throughout. To the right of the green section, there are vertical columns of black and purple squares. The grid appears to be a visual representation of data or a pattern.

For FDD
 
Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23700\_replaced([11098290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
   [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there are five columns, each containing a repeating pattern of a blue square, a black square, and then several light green squares.
The main body of the grid, starting from the sixth column from the left, is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are scattered squares of other colors:
- Purple squares appear in a somewhat diagonal pattern, with some columns having multiple purple squares.
- Red squares are interspersed, with a few appearing in the upper right section and a few in the lower middle section.
- Black squares are also present, breaking up the green and other colored squares.
The grid appears to be approximately 10 columns wide and 8 rows tall, though the exact dimensions are difficult to determine precisely due to the black background. The colors are distinct and the grid lines are clearly visible.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_985.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
  [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns.
The first column from the left is entirely filled with red squares.
The second column consists of gray squares.
The third column contains blue squares.
The remaining columns, from the fourth to the tenth, are predominantly filled with light green squares. Within these light green columns, there are scattered squares of black, purple, and red.
Specifically, the pattern of non-green squares in columns 4-10 is as follows:
- Column 4: Black squares appear in rows 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9.
- Column 5: Purple squares appear in rows 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10. Red squares appear in rows 2 and 6.
- Column 6: Black squares appear in rows 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9.
- Column 7: Purple squares appear in rows 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10.
- Column 8: Black squares appear in rows 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9. Red squares appear in rows 4 and 8.
- Column 9: Purple squares appear in rows 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10.
- Column 10: Black squares appear in rows 1, 3, 5, 7, and 9. Red squares appear in rows 1, 5, and 9.
The image can be represented as a markdown table:

| The ninth column from the left has 10 squares, alternating between purple and green, starting with purple at the top. Specifically, the squares are purple, green, purple, green, purple, green, purple, green, purple, green. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| The tenth column from the left contains 10 green squares. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| There are also some individual colored squares interspersed within the grid: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - In the seventh column, the 1st, 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th squares are red. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - In the seventh column, the 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, and 10th squares are green. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - In the ninth column, the 1st, 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th squares are purple. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - In the ninth column, the 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, and 10th squares are green. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Additionally, there are some red squares scattered in the main grid: |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - Row 2, Column 7 is red. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - Row 4, Column 7 is red. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - Row 6, Column 7 is red. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - Row 10, Column 7 is red. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| - Row 2, Column 9 is red. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| The image is a visual representation of a grid with a specific color pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_985.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| 5) for RB which is just above the LTE carrier bandwidth (RB number = dlBandwidth - (deltaRbNrLte >> 1) ) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
