1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
  [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns.
The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares.
The second column from the left contains 10 gray squares.
The third column from the left has 10 squares, alternating between blue and black. Specifically, the squares are blue, black, blue, black, blue, black, blue, black, blue, black.
The remaining eight columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, black, and purple squares.
Within these eight columns, there are also scattered red squares. Let's break down the pattern row by row, starting from the top:
Row 1: Light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 2: Light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 3: Light green, black, purple, light green, red, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 4: Light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 5: Light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 6: Light green, black, purple, light green, red, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 7: Light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 8: Light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 9: Light green, black, purple, light green, red, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
Row 10: Light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green, black, purple, light green.
The red squares appear in the following positions (row, column, counting from top-left as 1,1):
(3, 5)
(6, 5)
(9, 5)
(1, 9)
(4, 9)
(7, 9)
(10, 9)
The image does not contain any discernible text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_989.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
   [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column are eight blue squares, each in a different row. The remaining columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, black, and purple squares.
Specifically, starting from the third column from the left, the pattern is as follows:
- Column 3: Blue squares
- Column 4: Light green squares
- Column 5: Black squares
- Column 6: Light green squares
- Column 7: Purple squares
- Column 8: Light green squares
- Column 9: Black squares
- Column 10: Light green squares
- Column 11: Purple squares
- Column 12: Light green squares
- Column 13: Black squares
- Column 14: Light green squares
- Column 15: Purple squares
- Column 16: Light green squares
Within this pattern, there are also scattered red squares:
- Row 2, Column 14
- Row 4, Column 12
- Row 6, Column 14
- Row 8, Column 12
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 16 columns wide. The colors are distinct and the grid lines are clearly defined.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_989.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
  [image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns.
The first column from the left consists of 10 red squares.
The second column contains 10 gray squares.
The third column has 10 blue squares.
The remaining columns are filled with a pattern of light green, black, and purple squares.
Specifically, starting from the fourth column:
- Column 4: Alternating light green and black squares.
- Column 5: Alternating light green and purple squares.
- Column 6: Alternating light green and black squares.
- Column 7: Alternating light green and purple squares.
- Column 8: Alternating light green and black squares.
- Column 9: Alternating light green and purple squares.
- Column 10: Alternating light green and black squares.
There are also some individual colored squares interspersed within the light green areas:
- Row 2, Column 5: Red square
- Row 3, Column 5: Red square
- Row 4, Column 5: Red square
- Row 7, Column 5: Red square
- Row 8, Column 5: Red square
- Row 2, Column 7: Red square
- Row 8, Column 7: Red square
- Row 10, Column 7: Red square
The image is a visual representation of a grid with a specific color pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_989.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid has 10 rows and 16 columns. The leftmost column consists of 8 red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of 8 light gray squares. Next to the gray squares is a column of 8 blue squares. The remaining 13 columns are filled with light green squares, with some exceptions.
There are several red squares scattered within the light green area. Specifically, there are red squares in the following positions (row, column, counting from the top-left as 1,1):
- Row 2, Column 10
- Row 4, Column 7
- Row 7, Column 9
- Row 9, Column 5
- Row 10, Column 13
To the right of the light green area, there is a column of 8 black squares, alternating with 7 purple squares. The purple squares are located in rows 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 14.
The image is a visual representation, possibly a heatmap or a game board, with distinct colored regions and individual colored cells within a larger area.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_991.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

  [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column are eight blue squares, each in a different row. The remaining columns are filled with a repeating pattern of light green, black, and purple squares.
Specifically, starting from the third column from the left, the pattern is as follows:
- Column 3: Blue squares
- Column 4: Light green squares
- Column 5: Black squares
- Column 6: Light green squares
- Column 7: Purple squares
- Column 8: Light green squares
- Column 9: Black squares
- Column 10: Light green squares
- Column 11: Purple squares
- Column 12: Light green squares
- Column 13: Black squares
- Column 14: Light green squares
- Column 15: Purple squares
- Column 16: Light green squares
Within this pattern, there are also scattered red squares:
- Row 2, Column 14
- Row 4, Column 12
- Row 6, Column 14
- Row 8, Column 12
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 16 columns wide. The colors are distinct and the grid lines are clearly defined.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_993.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 10 rows and 10 columns of squares.
The leftmost column consists of 10 red squares.
The second column from the left is entirely made up of 10 gray squares.
The third column from the left contains 10 blue squares.
The fourth column from the left has 10 black squares.
The fifth column from the left is filled with 10 light green squares.
The sixth column from the left contains 10 purple squares.
The seventh column from the left is filled with 10 light green squares.
The eighth column from the left contains 10 black squares.
The ninth column from the left is filled with 10 light green squares.
The tenth column from the left contains 10 red squares.
There are also some individual colored squares interspersed within the grid:
- In the 5th row, 7th column, there is a red square.
- In the 6th row, 5th column, there is a red square.
- In the 7th row, 6th column, there is a red square.
- In the 9th row, 5th column, there is a red square.
- In the 10th row, 9th column, there is a red square.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_993.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
  [image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of this, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following this, there are five columns, each containing eight squares. These five columns exhibit a repeating pattern of colors: blue, black, light green, purple, black, light green, purple, and light green.
The remaining columns to the right are filled with light green squares, with occasional red, purple, and black squares interspersed. Specifically, there are red squares in the following positions (row, column, counting from top-left as 1,1): (1, 7), (2, 4), (4, 6), (5, 2), (7, 7), and (8, 7). Purple squares appear at (1, 5), (3, 5), (4, 5), (6, 5), (7, 5), and (8, 5). Black squares are present at (1, 6), (3, 6), (5, 6), (6, 6), (7, 6), and (8, 6).
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_993.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares. The grid is organized into rows and columns, with black lines separating each square.
On the left side of the grid, there is a vertical column of red squares, followed by a column of light gray squares, and then a column of blue squares.
The main body of the grid is filled with light green squares. Scattered within this green area are several red squares.
On the right side of the grid, there is a vertical column of black squares, followed by a column of purple squares.
The grid appears to be a visual representation of data, possibly a heatmap or a matrix.
The grid has 10 rows and 15 columns.
The colors present are red, light gray, blue, light green, black, and purple.
There is no discernible text in the image.

For FDD
 
Please, refer to  5G\_UP\_ALG\_23700\_replaced([11098290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km9bHvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 [image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is composed of 8 columns and 10 rows.
The first column from the left consists of 10 red squares.
The second column contains 10 gray squares.
The third column has 10 blue squares.
The remaining columns are filled with a mix of light green, black, and purple squares, with some red squares interspersed.
Specifically, the fourth column has 10 light green squares.
The fifth column has 5 black squares alternating with 5 light green squares.
The sixth column has 10 light green squares.
The seventh column has 5 black squares alternating with 5 light green squares.
The eighth column has 10 light green squares.
There are also several red squares scattered throughout the grid from the fourth column onwards.
In the fourth column, there is a red square in the first row.
In the fifth column, there is a red square in the third row.
In the seventh column, there is a red square in the second row and another in the fourth row.
In the eighth column, there is a red square in the first row, another in the third row, and a final one in the fifth row.
The grid appears to be a visual representation, possibly a heatmap or a diagram, where different colors signify different categories or values. The arrangement of colors suggests a structured pattern or data visualization.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_997.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares stacked vertically.
To the right of the red squares is a column of seven gray squares.
The next column contains a sequence of blue squares interspersed with black squares. Specifically, there are three blue squares, followed by a black square, then another blue square, a black square, and finally a blue square.
The subsequent columns are predominantly filled with light green squares. Within these green sections, there are scattered red, purple, and black squares.
Let's break down the grid by columns, starting from the column after the gray squares:
Column 4 (first column with green):
- Row 1: Red

The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide, with the first two columns being solid red and gray respectively. The remaining columns are a mix of colors as described above.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_997.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
        [image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray squares, there are eight blue squares arranged vertically. The remaining columns are predominantly filled with light green squares, interspersed with black, purple, and red squares.
Specifically, starting from the third column from the left (the blue column), the pattern of colors in the subsequent columns is as follows:
\* **Column 3 (Blue):** Eight blue squares.
\* **Column 4 (Green/Red/Black):** Light green squares with a single red square in the second row and a single black square in the first row.
\* **Column 5 (Green/Purple/Black):** Light green squares with purple squares in the third, fifth, and seventh rows, and black squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
\* **Column 6 (Green/Black):** All light green squares.
\* **Column 7 (Green/Purple/Black):** Light green squares with purple squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows, and black squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows.
\* **Column 8 (Green/Red):** Light green squares with a single red square in the first row.
\* **Column 9 (Green/Black):** All light green squares.
\* **Column 10 (Green/Purple):** Light green squares with purple squares in the first, third, fifth, and seventh rows.
\* **Column 11 (Green/Red):** Light green squares with a single red square in the eighth row.
\* **Column 12 (Green/Black):** All light green squares.
\* **Column 13 (Green/Purple):** Light green squares with purple squares in the second, fourth, sixth, and eighth rows.
\* **Column 14 (Green/Red):** Light green squares with a single red square in the sixth row.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 14 columns wide.
The image can be represented as a table:

| Row | Col 1 | Col 2 | Col 3 | Col 4 | Col 5 | Col 6 | Col 7 | Col 8 | Col 9 | Col 10 | Col 11 | Col 12 | Col 13 | Col 14 |

| 3 | Red | Gray | Blue | Green | Purple | Green | Black | Green | Green | Purple | Green | Green | Purple | Green |

###### 2.3.3.7.3 PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS

###### 2.3.3.7.3.0-1 PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS Overview (ID: 11098703)

**2.3.3.7.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11098707`)

PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS
For FDD, for LTE CRS puncturing NR PDSCH REs,  5G-L1-DL will puncture some REs in each RB of the PDSCH granted by LTE CRS REs  according to the following parameters (otherwise collision with LTE CRS REs, see lte-CRS-ToMatchAround IE in 3GPP 38.214 §5.1.4.2):

* the number of LTE CRS ports (see 36.211 §6.10.1.2).
* the 3GPP parameter LTE-CRS-vshift.
* the LTE carrier bandwidth provided by below table

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| LTE Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | LTE carrier bandwidth (RBs) |
| 20 MHz (FDD) | 100 |
| 15 MHz (FDD) | 75 |
| 10 MHz (FDD) | 50 |

* the position of each RB granted according to the central frequency of the NR carrier bandwidth (the mapping is different due to the fact in LTE the central frequency (that is DC carrier: Direct Current) is bypassed in DL baseband OFDM signal, see 36.211 §6.12).

The position of central frequency of the NR carrier bandwidth (ARFCN frequency (see 38.104) which is identical with the center frequency of LTE bandwidth, see 36.104) depends on downlink bandwidth. This position means the PRB index in the NR carrier bandwidth and the subcarrier index within this PRB as illustrated in the below table:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NR carrier Carrier Bandwidth (MHz) | NR carrier bandwidth (RBs) | ARFCN (or Dc) frequency |  |
| central RB | subcarrier of central RB |  |  |
| 20 MHz (FDD) | 106 | 53 | 0 |
| 15 MHz (FDD) | 79 | 39 | 6 |
| 10 MHz (FDD) | 52 | 26 | 0 |

Note: In NSA mode, RACH MSG2 is a PDSCH with mapping type A scheduled by DCI format 1\_0 and if it is scheduled with Nr slot carrying LTE CRS REs, as it is specified in 38.214 §5.1.6.2, dmrs-AdditionalPosition is set to 'pos2'. In order to avoid collision between DMRS REs and LTE CRS REs, for a PDSCH mapping type A, the number of symbols (ld in 38.211 §7.4.1.1.2) is set to 12 instead of 14, then the 2 additional DMRS are in OFDM symbols 6 and 9 instead of 7 and 11.

Note: The number of DMRS CDM group without data shall be equal to 2 (see 38.214 §5.1.6.2), the REs in the same symbol than DMRS (Front Load or additional) and not used by DMRS cannot be used for PDSCH data.

The REs (OFDM symbol lltecrs , subcarrier kltecrs ) which have to be withdrawn are listed with this formula (derived from the one for LTE CRS RE mapping in 36.211 §6.12 ):

###### 2.3.3.7.3.0-2 PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS Support PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS in L1 HW (ID: 11098711)

**2.3.3.7.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11098717`)

PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS
The PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS is supported for FDD and L1 HW as per table below:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| L1 HW | PDSCH punctured by LTE CRS 5G\_UP\_ALG\_14800\_replaced([11098703](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3WPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Loner | supported |
| Loki | supported |
| Thor | [CB008325-5G-BA] and Thor [End CB008325-5G-BA] |

subcarrier k range: [0..11] starting from first subcarrier of RB
deltaRbNrLte =difference between the number of RBs of the Nr carrier bandwidth and the number of RBs of the LTE carrier bandwidth (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_478\_replaced([11098711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3WvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For FDD
 
Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_485\_replaced([11098723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend:
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which extends to the right and downwards, is filled with a repeating pattern of light green, yellow, purple, and black squares. Specifically, starting from the column to the right of the blue squares, the pattern appears to be:
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
This pattern of light green, yellow/black, light green, purple/black repeats across the grid.
There is no discernible text in the image.
The grid can be represented as follows, where 'R' denotes red, 'G' denotes gray, 'B' denotes blue, 'L' denotes light green, 'Y' denotes yellow, 'P' denotes purple, and 'K' denotes black:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| 2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_485\_replaced([11098723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend:
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Next to the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid, which extends to the right and downwards, is filled with a repeating pattern of light green, yellow, and purple squares, interspersed with black squares.
Specifically, starting from the fourth column from the left:
- The first row has a light green square, followed by a yellow square, then a light green square, a black square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, and a yellow square.
- The second row has a light green square, a light green square, a yellow square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, a yellow square, and a light green square.
- The third row has a light green square, a yellow square, a light green square, a black square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, and a yellow square.
- The fourth row has a light green square, a light green square, a yellow square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, a yellow square, and a light green square.
- The fifth row has a light green square, a yellow square, a light green square, a black square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, and a yellow square.
- The sixth row has a light green square, a light green square, a yellow square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, a yellow square, and a light green square.
- The seventh row has a light green square, a yellow square, a light green square, a black square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, and a yellow square.
- The eighth row has a light green square, a light green square, a yellow square, a light green square, a purple square, a light green square, a yellow square, and a light green square.
The pattern of yellow and purple squares within the light green background appears to be in a diagonal or staggered arrangement. There are also vertical columns of black and purple squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1017.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid consists of eight columns, each containing eight squares. These columns exhibit a repeating pattern of colors:
\* **Column 1 (after blue):** Alternating green and yellow squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 2:** Alternating green and black squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 3:** Alternating green and purple squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 4:** Alternating green and yellow squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 5:** Alternating green and black squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 6:** Alternating green and purple squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 7:** Alternating green and yellow squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 8:** Alternating green and black squares, starting with green.
The overall impression is a structured, colorful pattern within a grid. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1017.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid consists of a repeating pattern of colors. The majority of the squares in this section are light green. Interspersed within the light green squares are columns of yellow, black, and purple squares.
Specifically, starting from the column to the right of the blue squares:
- The first column is entirely light green.
- The second column has a yellow square in the first row, followed by light green squares for the rest of the rows.
- The third column is entirely light green.
- The fourth column has a purple square in the second row, a black square in the third row, a purple square in the fourth row, a black square in the fifth row, a purple square in the sixth row, and light green squares in the seventh and eighth rows.
- The fifth column is entirely light green.
- The sixth column has a yellow square in the first row, light green squares in the second and third rows, a yellow square in the fourth row, light green squares in the fifth and sixth rows, and a yellow square in the seventh and eighth rows.
- The seventh column is entirely light green.
- The eighth column has a purple square in the second row, a black square in the third row, a purple square in the fourth row, a black square in the fifth row, a purple square in the sixth row, and light green squares in the seventh and eighth rows.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The remaining 7 columns exhibit a patterned arrangement of light green, yellow, purple, and black squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1017.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid consists of eight columns, each containing eight squares. These columns exhibit a repeating pattern of colors:
\* **Column 1 (after blue):** Alternating green and yellow squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 2:** Alternating green and black squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 3:** Alternating green and purple squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 4:** Alternating green and yellow squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 5:** Alternating green and black squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 6:** Alternating green and purple squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 7:** Alternating green and yellow squares, starting with green.
\* **Column 8:** Alternating green and black squares, starting with green.
The overall impression is a structured, colorful pattern within a grid. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1019.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except the central RB )
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which extends to the right and downwards, is filled with a repeating pattern of light green, yellow, purple, and black squares. Specifically, starting from the column to the right of the blue squares, the pattern appears to be:
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
This pattern of light green, yellow/black, light green, purple/black repeats across the grid.
There is no discernible text in the image.
The grid can be represented as follows, where 'R' denotes red, 'G' denotes gray, 'B' denotes blue, 'L' denotes light green, 'Y' denotes yellow, 'P' denotes purple, and 'K' denotes black:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
| R | G | B | L | Y | L | P | L | Y |
| R | G | B | L | K | L | K | L | K |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| 3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth) |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

However, the exact repetition and placement of the yellow and black squares vary. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

For FDD
Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_485\_replaced([11098723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend:
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is arranged in rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which is much larger, is filled with a repeating pattern of green, yellow, purple, and black squares. Specifically, starting from the column to the right of the blue squares, the pattern appears to be:
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
This pattern repeats across the remaining columns of the grid. The grid appears to be 8 rows high and approximately 12 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1022.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid consists of a repeating pattern of colors. There are eight rows and eight columns in this section. The colors present are green, yellow, purple, and black.
The pattern appears to be as follows:
- The first column of this section (ninth column overall) is entirely green.
- The second column (tenth column overall) has alternating yellow and black squares, starting with yellow at the top.
- The third column (eleventh column overall) is entirely green.
- The fourth column (twelfth column overall) has alternating purple and black squares, starting with purple at the top.
- The fifth column (thirteenth column overall) is entirely green.
- The sixth column (fourteenth column overall) has alternating yellow and black squares, starting with yellow at the top.
- The seventh column (fifteenth column overall) is entirely green.
- The eighth column (sixteenth column overall) has alternating purple and black squares, starting with purple at the top.
The grid has a total of 8 rows and 16 columns. The colors are arranged in a structured, repeating pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1022.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
 3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Next to the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid consists of rows and columns of light green, yellow, and purple squares, interspersed with black squares.
Specifically, starting from the column to the right of the blue squares, the pattern appears to be:
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
- A column of light green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
The grid has 10 columns of colored squares to the right of the initial red, gray, and blue columns. The rows are consistent across these columns.
The overall impression is a visual representation of data or a pattern, possibly a simplified heatmap or a coded diagram. There is no discernible text in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1022.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_485\_replaced([11098723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend:
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid consists of a repeating pattern of colors. There are eight rows and eight columns in this section. The colors present are green, yellow, purple, and black.
The pattern appears to be as follows:
- The first column of this section (ninth column overall) is entirely green.
- The second column (tenth column overall) has alternating yellow and black squares, starting with yellow at the top.
- The third column (eleventh column overall) is entirely green.
- The fourth column (twelfth column overall) has alternating purple and black squares, starting with purple at the top.
- The fifth column (thirteenth column overall) is entirely green.
- The sixth column (fourteenth column overall) has alternating yellow and black squares, starting with yellow at the top.
- The seventh column (fifteenth column overall) is entirely green.
- The eighth column (sixteenth column overall) has alternating purple and black squares, starting with purple at the top.
The grid has a total of 8 rows and 16 columns. The colors are arranged in a structured, repeating pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1024.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which is much larger, is filled with a repeating pattern of green, yellow, and purple squares, interspersed with black squares.
Specifically, starting from the fourth column from the left (the first column after the blue squares):
- The first row has a yellow square, followed by a green square, then a black square, then a green square, then a yellow square, then a green square, then a black square, then a green square, and finally a yellow square.
- The second row has a green square, followed by a yellow square, then a green square, then a black square, then a green square, then a yellow square, then a green square, then a black square, and finally a green square.
- The third row is identical to the first row.
- The fourth row is identical to the second row.
- This pattern of alternating rows (first row pattern, second row pattern, first row pattern, second row pattern, etc.) continues for all eight rows of the main grid.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide. The first three columns are solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The remaining nine columns are filled with the repeating pattern of green, yellow, black, and purple squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1024.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid, which extends to the right, is filled with a repeating pattern of green, yellow, and purple squares, interspersed with black squares. Specifically, starting from the column to the right of the blue squares, the pattern appears to be:
\* **Column 1 (after blue):** Green squares.
\* **Column 2:** Yellow squares.
\* **Column 3:** Green squares.
\* **Column 4:** Black squares.
\* **Column 5:** Green squares.
\* **Column 6:** Purple squares.
\* **Column 7:** Green squares.
\* **Column 8:** Yellow squares.
\* **Column 9:** Green squares.
\* **Column 10:** Black squares.
\* **Column 11:** Green squares.
\* **Column 12:** Purple squares.
\* **Column 13:** Green squares.
\* **Column 14:** Yellow squares.
\* **Column 15:** Green squares.
This pattern repeats across the rows. The grid has 8 rows and approximately 15 columns of colored squares, excluding the initial red, gray, and blue columns. The overall impression is a visual representation of data or a pattern, possibly a heatmap or a coded sequence.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1024.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which is much larger, is filled with a repeating pattern of green, yellow, and purple squares, interspersed with black squares.
Specifically, starting from the fourth column from the left (the first column after the blue squares):
- The first row has a yellow square, followed by a green square, then a black square, then a green square, then a yellow square, then a green square, then a black square, then a green square, and finally a yellow square.
- The second row has a green square, followed by a yellow square, then a green square, then a black square, then a green square, then a yellow square, then a green square, then a black square, and finally a green square.
- The third row is identical to the first row.
- The fourth row is identical to the second row.
- This pattern of alternating rows (first row pattern, second row pattern, first row pattern, second row pattern, etc.) continues for all eight rows of the main grid.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide. The first three columns are solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The remaining nine columns are filled with the repeating pattern of green, yellow, black, and purple squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1026.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is arranged in rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which is much larger, is filled with a repeating pattern of green, yellow, purple, and black squares. Specifically, starting from the column to the right of the blue squares, the pattern appears to be:
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating yellow and black squares.
- A column of green squares.
- A column with alternating purple and black squares.
This pattern repeats across the remaining columns of the grid. The grid appears to be 8 rows high and approximately 12 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1026.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following this is a column of eight blue squares. The remaining columns are filled with a repeating pattern of green, yellow, purple, and black squares.
Specifically, starting from the fourth column from the left, the pattern appears to be:
- Column 4: Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow
- Column 5: Purple, Black, Purple, Black, Purple, Black, Purple, Black
- Column 6: Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow
- Column 7: Purple, Black, Purple, Black, Purple, Black, Purple, Black
- Column 8: Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow, Green, Yellow
The grid has 8 rows and 8 columns of colored squares.
The table representation of the colored squares is as follows:

| Row | Col 1 | Col 2 | Col 3 | Col 4 | Col 5 | Col 6 | Col 7 | Col 8 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Red | Gray | Blue | Green | Purple | Green | Purple | Green |
| 2 | Red | Gray | Blue | Yellow | Black | Yellow | Black | Yellow |
| 3 | Red | Gray | Blue | Green | Purple | Green | Purple | Green |
| 4 | Red | Gray | Blue | Yellow | Black | Yellow | Black | Yellow |
| 5 | Red | Gray | Blue | Green | Purple | Green | Purple | Green |
| 6 | Red | Gray | Blue | Yellow | Black | Yellow | Black | Yellow |
| 7 | Red | Gray | Blue | Green | Purple | Green | Purple | Green |
| 8 | Red | Gray | Blue | Yellow | Black | Yellow | Black | Yellow |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

The third column has 8 blue squares. The remaining 7 columns are filled with a repeating pattern of green, yellow, purple, and black squares. Specifically, in columns 4 through 10, the pattern is: green, yellow, green, purple, green, black, green, yellow, green, purple, green, black, and so on, repeating every 6 squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1026.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

###### 2.3.3.7.3.1.3 One LTE CRS Port

###### 2.3.3.7.3.1.3-1 One LTE CRS Port

**2.3.3.7.3.1.3-1.0-3**  (ID: `11098941`)

One LTE CRS Port
For each L1 HW and for FDD, the different configurations where the LTE CRS rate puncturing needs to be applied are:

* function of the value of the number of LTE CRS ports and the cell-specific frequency shift,
* depicted by figures legends details provided in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_485\_replaced([11098723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),
* supported as in table below:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | no. of LTE CRS port = 2 5G\_UP\_ALG\_487\_replaced([11098732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knAeYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | no. of LTE CRS port = 4 5G\_UP\_ALG\_494\_replaced([11098783](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knAeaPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | no. of LTE CRS port = 1 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12878\_replaced([11098835](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knAecPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) |
| Loner | supported | supported | - |
| Loki | supported | supported | supported |
| Thor | supported | supported | [CB009029-5G-A] supported [End CB009029-5G-A] |

---

1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following this is a column with alternating blue and black squares, starting with blue at the top.
The remaining columns are filled with a pattern of light green, purple, and yellow squares, interspersed with black squares.
Specifically, the grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide.
Here's a breakdown of the grid's contents, row by row, from left to right:
Row 1: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 2: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
Row 3: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 4: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
Row 5: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 6: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
Row 7: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 8: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
The image does not contain any discernible text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1029.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 
2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (including central RB with subcarrier 0 -even number number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following this, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several distinct patterns:
\* **Yellow squares:** These appear sporadically. There is one in the second row, fourth column from the left (after the blue column). Another is in the fourth row, sixth column. A third is in the seventh row, fourth column. The final yellow square is in the eighth row, eighth column.
\* **Purple squares:** These are arranged in vertical lines. One vertical line of purple squares is in the fifth column, appearing in rows two, four, six, and eight. Another vertical line of purple squares is in the seventh column, appearing in rows one, three, five, and seven.
\* **Black squares:** These are interspersed with the purple squares, forming vertical lines. A vertical line of black squares is in the sixth column, appearing in rows one, three, five, and seven. Another vertical line of black squares is in the eighth column, appearing in rows two, four, six, and eight.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The remaining seven columns are primarily light green with the aforementioned colored squares interspersed.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1029.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid is filled with light green squares, with some interspersed black, yellow, and purple squares.
Let's break down the grid from left to right, row by row, starting from the top:
**Column 1 (Red):** All 8 squares are red.
**Column 2 (Gray):** All 8 squares are gray.
**Column 3 (Blue):** All 8 squares are blue.
**Columns 4 through 11 (Main Grid):**
\* **Row 1:** Light green, black, yellow, light green, black, light green, black, light green, black, light green, yellow.
\* **Row 2:** Light green, black, light green, light green, purple, light green, black, light green, purple, light green, light green.
\* **Row 3:** Light green, black, light green, light green, light green, light green, black, light green, light green, purple, light green.
\* **Row 4:** Light green, black, light green, yellow, light green, light green, black, light green, light green, light green, light green.
\* **Row 5:** Light green, black, light green, light green, purple, light green, black, light green, purple, light green, light green.
\* **Row 6:** Light green, black, light green, light green, light green, light green, black, light green, light green, purple, light green.
\* **Row 7:** Light green, black, light green, yellow, light green, light green, black, light green, light green, light green, light green.
\* **Row 8:** Light green, black, light green, light green, purple, light green, black, light green, purple, light green, yellow.
The image does not contain any discernible text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1029.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

The remaining part of the grid is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several distinct patterns:
\* **Yellow squares:** These appear sporadically. There is one in the second row, fourth column from the left (after the blue column). Another is in the fourth row, sixth column. A third is in the seventh row, fourth column. The final yellow square is in the eighth row, eighth column.
\* **Purple squares:** These are arranged in vertical lines. One vertical line of purple squares is in the fifth column, appearing in rows two, four, six, and eight. Another vertical line of purple squares is in the seventh column, appearing in rows one, three, five, and seven.
\* **Black squares:** These are interspersed with the purple squares, forming vertical lines. A vertical line of black squares is in the sixth column, appearing in rows one, three, five, and seven. Another vertical line of black squares is in the eighth column, appearing in rows two, four, six, and eight.

The remaining seven columns are primarily light green with the aforementioned colored squares interspersed.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1031.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of 8 red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of 8 gray squares. Following this, there is a column of 8 blue squares.
The rest of the grid is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this green area, there are several other colored squares interspersed.
Specifically, in the column immediately to the right of the blue squares, there are black squares at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
In the next column to the right, there are yellow squares at rows 1 and 5, and purple squares at rows 3, 7, and 9.
In the column after that, there are black squares at rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
In the next column, there are yellow squares at rows 2 and 6, and purple squares at rows 4 and 8.
In the column to the right of that, there are black squares at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
In the final visible column of the grid, there are yellow squares at rows 1 and 7, and purple squares at rows 3 and 5.
The grid appears to be 9 rows high and 9 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The remaining columns are a mix of green, black, yellow, and purple squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1031.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following this, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this green area, there are several scattered squares of other colors:
- Yellow squares appear in the following positions (row, column, starting from top-left of the entire grid): (1, 4), (3, 6), (5, 4), (7, 7), (8, 4).
- Purple squares appear in the following positions: (2, 5), (3, 7), (4, 5), (5, 7), (6, 5), (7, 5), (8, 7).
- Black squares appear in the following positions: (2, 4), (4, 4), (6, 4), (8, 5).
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The remaining columns are primarily green with interspersed yellow, purple, and black squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1031.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of 8 red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of 8 gray squares. Following this, there is a column of 8 blue squares.
The rest of the grid is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this green area, there are several other colored squares interspersed.
Specifically, in the column immediately to the right of the blue squares, there are black squares at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
In the next column to the right, there are yellow squares at rows 1 and 5, and purple squares at rows 3, 7, and 9.
In the column after that, there are black squares at rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
In the next column, there are yellow squares at rows 2 and 6, and purple squares at rows 4 and 8.
In the column to the right of that, there are black squares at rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
In the final visible column of the grid, there are yellow squares at rows 1 and 7, and purple squares at rows 3 and 5.
The grid appears to be 9 rows high and 9 columns wide, with the first three columns being solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The remaining columns are a mix of green, black, yellow, and purple squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1033.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a vertical block of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a vertical block of eight gray squares. Further to the right, there is a vertical column of eight blue squares.
The majority of the grid is filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are scattered purple, yellow, and black squares.
Specifically, the purple squares appear in vertical columns, interspersed with black squares. There are also individual yellow squares scattered throughout the green area.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and approximately 12 columns wide.
The colors present are red, gray, blue, light green, purple, yellow, and black.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1033.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns. On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column is a column of eight blue squares. The remaining area of the grid is filled with light green squares, interspersed with black, purple, and yellow squares.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 16 columns wide.
Here's a breakdown of the colors in each column, from left to right:
\* **Column 1:** 8 red squares.
\* **Column 2:** 8 gray squares.
\* **Column 3:** 8 blue squares.
\* **Columns 4-16:** Primarily light green squares, with the following exceptions:
\* **Column 4:** Contains black squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
\* **Column 5:** Contains purple squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7, and a yellow square in row 2.
\* **Column 6:** Contains black squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
\* **Column 7:** Contains purple squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a yellow square in row 1.
\* **Column 8:** Contains black squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
\* **Column 9:** Contains purple squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7, and a yellow square in row 3.
\* **Column 10:** Contains black squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
\* **Column 11:** Contains purple squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a yellow square in row 4.
\* **Column 12:** Contains black squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
\* **Column 13:** Contains purple squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7, and a yellow square in row 5.
\* **Column 14:** Contains black squares in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
\* **Column 15:** Contains purple squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a yellow square in row 6.
\* **Column 16:** Contains black squares in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8, and a yellow square in row 7.
The image is a visual representation of a grid with a specific color pattern.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1033.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
 
 
4) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following this, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid, which extends to the right, is filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several scattered squares of other colors:
- There are four yellow squares.
- There are six purple squares.
- There are five black squares.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide.
The arrangement of colors suggests a pattern or a visual representation of data. The red, gray, and blue columns on the left might represent categories or initial states, while the light green area with interspersed colors could represent further data points or outcomes.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1033.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
 
 
5) for RB which is just above the LTE carrier bandwidth (RB number = dlBandwidth - (deltaRbNrLte >> 1) )
 
[image-4][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column with seven blue squares, with the bottom square in this column being black.
The remaining columns are filled with a repeating pattern of colors. The majority of the squares in these columns are light green. Interspersed within the light green squares are columns of black and purple squares. Specifically, there are four columns that alternate between light green and black squares, with a single purple square placed in the middle of each black square.
The bottom row of the grid has a different pattern. Starting from the leftmost column, the bottom squares are red, gray, blue, and then yellow. The subsequent squares in the bottom row are light green, with a single purple square in the fifth column from the left, and a yellow square in the last column.
The grid appears to be approximately 8 rows high and 10 columns wide, with the colors arranged in a structured, patterned manner.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1033.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

On the left side of the grid, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares.

[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several scattered squares of yellow and purple, as well as some black squares.
Specifically, the grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide.
Let's break down the colors row by row, starting from the top:

For FDD
 
Please, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_485\_replaced([11098723](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km_3XvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the legend:
 
1) RBs where the subcarriers are below the ARFCN frequency
 
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid is filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several scattered squares of yellow and purple.
Specifically, the yellow squares appear in the following positions (row, column, counting from the top left of the entire grid, starting at 1):
- Row 2, Column 5
- Row 3, Column 4
- Row 5, Column 6
- Row 7, Column 4
- Row 8, Column 7
The purple squares appear in the following positions:
- Row 2, Column 6
- Row 3, Column 7
- Row 4, Column 6
- Row 5, Column 7
- Row 6, Column 6
- Row 7, Column 7
- Row 8, Column 6
There are also black squares interspersed within the light green area, forming vertical lines. These black squares are located at:
- Column 6, Rows 2, 4, 6, 8
- Column 8, Rows 2, 4, 6, 8
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide. The colors are distinct and the grid lines are clearly defined.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1039.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 2) RBs where the subcarriers are above the ARFCN frequency (except central RB)
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
The leftmost column consists of eight red squares. To the right of the red squares is a column of eight gray squares. Following this is a column with alternating blue and black squares, starting with blue at the top.
The remaining columns are filled with a pattern of light green, purple, and yellow squares, interspersed with black squares.
Specifically, the grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide.
Here's a breakdown of the grid's contents, row by row, from left to right:
Row 1: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 2: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
Row 3: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 4: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
Row 5: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 6: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
Row 7: Red, Gray, Blue, Green, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green
Row 8: Red, Gray, Black, Green, Purple, Green, Black, Green, Yellow, Green
The image does not contain any discernible text.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1039.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
3) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 0 (even number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-2][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which occupies the majority of the image, is filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several other colored squares interspersed:
- There are four yellow squares, located at positions (row 2, column 4), (row 4, column 6), (row 6, column 4), and (row 8, column 7).
- There are five purple squares, located at positions (row 3, column 5), (row 5, column 5), (row 7, column 5), (row 3, column 7), and (row 5, column 7).
- There are five black squares, located at positions (row 2, column 5), (row 4, column 5), (row 6, column 5), (row 8, column 5), and (row 2, column 7).
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide. The black background surrounds the grid on all sides.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1039.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
 
4) central RB if the subcarrier of the central RB is equal to 6 (odd number of RBs for the carrier bandwidth)
 
[image-3][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of eight red squares. To the right of the red column, there is a column of eight gray squares. Following the gray column, there is a column of eight blue squares.
The remaining part of the grid, which extends to the right, is filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are several other colored squares interspersed:
\* **Yellow squares:** There are four yellow squares. One is in the 3rd row from the top, 4th column from the left (within the green area). Another is in the 6th row, 4th column. A third is in the 2nd row, 7th column. The fourth is in the 7th row, 7th column.
\* **Purple squares:** There are eight purple squares. They appear in a vertical pattern in the 5th column from the left (within the green area), with black squares separating them. Specifically, they are in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8. There are also purple squares in the 9th column, in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7.
\* **Black squares:** There are eight black squares. They are located in the 5th column from the left (within the green area), interspersed with the purple squares, in rows 1, 3, 5, and 7. There are also black squares in the 9th column, in rows 2, 4, 6, and 8.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 10 columns wide in total, with the first three columns on the left being solid red, gray, and blue respectively. The subsequent columns contain the green, yellow, purple, and black squares.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1039.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
 
 
5) for RB which is just above the LTE carrier bandwidth (RB number = dlBandwidth - (deltaRbNrLte >> 1) )
 
 
 [image-4][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the far left, there is a column of 8 red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of 8 gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of 8 blue squares.
The rest of the grid, which extends to the right and downwards, is predominantly filled with light green squares. Within this light green area, there are vertical stripes of purple squares interspersed with black squares. Specifically, there are four such vertical stripes. The first purple stripe is in the 5th column from the left, containing 5 purple squares separated by black squares. The second purple stripe is in the 7th column from the left, also containing 5 purple squares separated by black squares. The third purple stripe is in the 9th column from the left, with 5 purple squares separated by black squares. The fourth purple stripe is in the 11th column from the left, with 5 purple squares separated by black squares.
In the bottom row, in the 10th column from the left, there is a single yellow square.
The grid appears to be 8 rows high and 12 columns wide, with the colored squares filling most of the visible grid area. The black background surrounds the grid.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1039.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4]

11098963:
PDSCH additional DMRS shift
For FDD and Loner, Loki [CB008325-5G-BA] and Thor [End CB008325-5G-BA]  L1 HWs:
For the additional DMRS shift scheme, if one additional DMRS are configured,  5G-L1-DL will shift the additonal DMRS from OFDM symbol 11 to OFDM symbol 12 (see 38.211 §7.4.1.1.2).
 
Note:
This case happens for a PDSCH mapping type A which is scheduled in MBSFN subframe with one additional DMRS. There is no LTE CRS rate matching, neither LTE CRS puncturing for this PDSCH.
 
The RE mapping of the DMRS shift is shown as below figures.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a vertical arrangement of six colored rectangles against a black background. From top to bottom, the colors are red, white, lime green, black, blue, and purple. Each rectangle is solid and appears to be of similar size and shape.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1047.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid of colored squares against a black background. The grid is organized into rows and columns.
On the left side of the grid, there is a vertical column of red squares. To the right of the red squares, there is a column of light gray squares. Following the gray squares, there is a column of blue squares.
The majority of the grid is filled with light green squares, forming a large rectangular area.
On the right side of the grid, there is a vertical column of black squares interspersed with purple squares. The pattern in this column is black, purple, black, purple, black, purple, black, purple.
The grid appears to be approximately 8 rows high and 20 columns wide. The colors are distinct and the grid lines are clearly visible. There is no text present in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1047.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

###### 2.3.3.7.5.0-1 PDSCH flexible ratematching due to LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH , LTE PSS/SSS/repeated M-PBCH and CAT-M M-PDCCH and M-PDSCH for user data Supported L1 HW configurations (ID: 11098971)

**2.3.3.7.5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11098976`)

PDSCH flexible ratematching due to LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH , LTE PSS/SSS/repeated M-PBCH and CAT-M M-PDCCH and M-PDSCH for user data
For FDD DSS and Loner, Loki [CB008325-5G-BA] and Thor [End CB008325-5G-BA]  L1 HWs:

All the figures in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_509\_replaced([11098979](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knBskPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for flexible ratematching for LTE PSS/SSS/PBCH) are supported and applicable.
