11100334:
Precoding and antenna port mapping
For TDD FR1, DU [CB009514-F] and Radio (for 7-2e) [End CB009514-F] shall support 8 port CSI-RS transmission when dlMimoMode indicates "8x4 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing"
 
- Per 3GPP :
\* ports 3000-3003 shall be sent on polarization +45 as type I PMI indicates for the first half of the streams in output of the precoding for PDSCH ports (outpout of precoding) 1000-1003
\* ports 3004-3007 shall be sent on polarization -45 as type I PMI indicates for the second half of the of the streams in output of the precoding for PDSCH ports (output of precoding) 1004-1007
 
 - Therefore on each symbol, as result of L1 and RU transmission, pairs of CSI-RS ports shall be transmitted on 4 distinct subpanels transmitting in same direction:
\* Each port of the (p0, p4) is sent on separate polarizations of subpanel 0
\* Each port of the (p1, p5) is sent on separate polarizations of subpanel 1
\* Each port of the (p2, p6) is sent on separate polarizations of subpanel 2
\* Each port of the (p3, p7) is sent on separate polarizations of subpanel 3
 
   - L1 shall supports row8  in TS 38.211 Table 7.4.1.5.3-1 for each symbol transmission.
 
Below is updated to be consistent to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_2506\_replaced([11290062](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TqCbavvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Regarding the patternId of the beam to be used, L2 shall send different patternIds for each symbol, so that RU transmission of the 8 ports are compliant with 3GPP (38.211 Table 7.4.1.5.3-1).
L2 transmission of pattern Ids is described in 5G\_L2\_494\_replaced([11156008](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248vSvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
 
Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more explanations about mapping to polarization (CPRI, eCPRI).
[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram with four rectangular boxes arranged in a 2x2 grid within a larger rounded rectangle. Each box contains text in yellow font. The boxes are dark blue with rounded corners. The background appears to be a grid.
The text in the boxes is as follows:
\* **Top Left Box:** Port (1,5): subpanel 1 (+45,-45)
\* **Top Right Box:** Port (3,7): subpanel 3 (+45,-45)
\* **Bottom Left Box:** Port (0,4): subpanel 0 (+45,-45)
\* **Bottom Right Box:** Port (2,6): subpanel 2 (+45,-45)[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1330.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
 [image-1][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating the architecture of a 64TRX Radio Unit (RU). The diagram shows the flow of signals from the baseband processing to the antenna elements.
The diagram is organized into several columns, representing different stages of signal processing and distribution:
\* **Baseband BB**: This column on the far left indicates the input from the baseband processing. It shows two inputs labeled "(e)AxC0" and "(e)AxC2".
\* **Ports**: This column shows the ports that connect the baseband signals to the digital RF processing. There are four ports labeled "port0", "port1", "port2", and "port3".
\* **Digital RF**: This is a large green block representing the digital radio frequency processing. It contains several sub-blocks labeled with frequency ranges (e.g., "w0-3", "w8-11", etc.) and processing elements represented by circles with crosses inside, labeled with "alpha" for ports 2 and 3.
\* **K TRX**: This blue block represents the transceiver units. Each block is labeled with a TRX identifier (e.g., "TRX0-3", "TRX8-11", etc.). Each TRX block is associated with a symbol representing a wireless signal.
\* **L antenna elements**: This column on the right shows the antenna elements, represented by gray cloud-like shapes pointing towards the TRX units. Each antenna element is associated with a "subpanel" and a polarization value, e.g., "subpanel 0(+45)", "subpanel 1(+45)", "subpanel 2(+45)", and "subpanel 3(+45)".
There are also annotations explaining the signal processing logic:
\* "p2=p0*alpha" and "p3=p0*alpha" indicate how signals are processed for ports 2 and 3.
\* "Symbol0: alpha=1" and "Symbol1: alpha=-1" define the values of alpha for different symbols.
The title of the diagram states: "Example of 64TRX RU; (N1,N2)=(2,2); subpanels 0..3: polarization +45 (=32TRXs)". This indicates that the RU supports 64 TRX units, with a configuration of (N1,N2)=(2,2), and that subpanels 0 through 3 are used with +45 polarization, totaling 32 TRX units.
The diagram visually represents how baseband signals are routed through different ports, processed in the digital RF stage, transmitted by the transceivers, and finally radiated by the antenna elements with specific polarization. The use of "alpha" suggests a mechanism for signal manipulation or phase shifting.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1330.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
 
  [image-2][image\_desc]The image is a block diagram illustrating the architecture of a 64TRX Radio Unit (RU). The diagram shows the flow of signals from the baseband processing unit to the antenna elements, organized by streams, digital RF components, TRX (Transceiver) units, and antenna elements.
The diagram is structured into several columns:
\* **Baseband BB**: This column represents the baseband processing unit. It shows two input lines labeled "(e)AxC1" and "(e)AxC3". These lines connect to ports labeled "port4" and "port5" respectively. There are also lines labeled "port5" and "port7" originating from "(e)AxC3", with "alpha" symbols indicating some form of signal manipulation or weighting. Below these lines, there are definitions for "p6=p4*alpha", "p7=p5*alpha", and "Symbol0: alpha=1", "Symbol1: alpha=-1".
\* **M streams**: This column is a header indicating the concept of "M streams".
\* **digital RF**: This column represents the digital Radio Frequency processing. It contains several blocks labeled with "w" followed by a range of numbers (e.g., "w0-3", "w8-11", "W16-19", "W24-27", "w4-7", "w12-15", "₩20-23", "w28-31"). Each of these blocks has a circular symbol with a cross inside, suggesting a processing or mixing operation. These blocks are connected to the ports from the baseband section.
\* **K TRX**: This column represents the Transceiver units. It contains blocks labeled with "TRX" followed by a range of numbers (e.g., "TRX32-35", "TRX40-43", "TRX48-51", "TRX56-59", "TRX36-39", "TRX44-47", "TRX52-55", "TRX60-63"). Each TRX block is connected to a corresponding digital RF block.
\* **L antenna elements**: This column represents the antenna elements. It shows stylized representations of antenna beams, each labeled with a "subpanel" number and a polarization value (e.g., "subpanel 0(-45)", "subpanel 1(-45)", "subpanel 2(-45)", "subpanel 3(-45)"). Each subpanel is connected to a corresponding TRX unit via a wireless symbol.
The title of the diagram states: "Example of 64TRX RU; (N1,N2)=(2,2); subpanels 0..3: polarization -45 (=32TRXs)". This indicates that the RU supports 64 TRX units, is configured with parameters (N1,N2) as (2,2), and has 4 subpanels (0 to 3) operating at a polarization of -45 degrees, totaling 32 TRX units.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1330.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2]
 
[CB009514-F]
Note:
- Add link to NE 5G-L1-DL (Radio) as CSI-RS precoding and antenna porting is moved to Radio(L1) for eCPRI 7-2e. (Note kept only for this version)
-Mapping between stream and polarization is done in Radio(L1) internally according to table below, and no eAxC links are involved, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23429\_replaced([11100910](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
[End CB009514-F]
 
File: CSI-RS\_ports\_mapping\_to\_subpanels.pptx
Folder:

###### 2.3.4.2.1.1.0-7 Precoding and antenna port mapping 8 port CSI-RS : antenna port mapping for CPRI TDD **** (ID: 11100338)

**2.3.4.2.1.1.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11100345`)

Precoding and antenna port mapping
For TDD FR1, for L1 Loner, Loki HW [CB010575-D] and Thor HW  [End CB010575-D] 8 port CSI-RS is transmitted when dlMimoMode indicates "8x4 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing"

For CPRI case, Table 1 applies to both symbols as 2 consecutive symbols are needed.

CPRI AxC mapping to polarization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Symbol 0 | AxC | Polarization |
| 3000 | 0 | +45 |
| 3001 | 2 | +45 |
| 3004 | 1 | -45 |
| 3005 | 3 | -45 |

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more explanations about mapping to polarization (CPRI, eCPRI).

---

###### 2.3.4.2.1.1.0-8 Precoding and antenna port mapping 8 port CSI-RS : antenna port mapping for eCPRI TDD **** (ID: 11100349)

**2.3.4.2.1.1.0-8.0-4**  (ID: `13438820`)

Precoding and antenna port mapping
For TDD FR1, for L1 Loner, Loki, Thor, Thor (Radio 7-2e) [CB016061-B1] GPU HW [End CB016061-B1], DU and Radio (for 7-2e) shall support 8 port CSI-RS transmission when dlMimoMode indicates "8x4 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing"

For eCPRI case, Table below indicates 1st symbol and 2nd symbol eAxC mapping to polarization as 2 consecutive symbols are needed.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Symbol 0 | eAxC | Polarization | PatternId CSI-RS beam Bi symbol 0 |
| 3000 | 0 | +45 | Pi0\_symb0 (combine 2 ports 0,2 with alpha=1) |
| 3001 | 2 | +45 | Pi1\_symb0 (combine 2 ports 1,3 with alpha=1) |
| 3004 | 1 | -45 | Pi2\_symb0(combine 2 ports 4,6 with alpha=1) |
| 3005 | 3 | -45 | Pi3\_symb0 (combine 2 ports 5,7 with alpha=1) |

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Symbol 1 | eAxC | Polarization | PatternId CSI-RS beam Bi symbol 1 |
| 3000 | 0 | +45 | Pi0\_symb1 (combine 2 ports 0,2 with alpha = -1) |
| 3001 | 2 | +45 | Pi1\_symb1 (combine 2 ports 1,3 with alpha = -1) |
| 3004 | 1 | -45 | Pi2\_symb1 (combine 2 ports 4,6 with alpha = -1) |
| 3005 | 3 | -45 | Pi3\_symb1 (combine 2 ports 5,7 with alpha = -1) |

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more explanations about mapping to polarization (CPRI, eCPRI).

Note:

-For eCPRI 7-2e, mapping between stream and polarization is done in Radio(L1) internally according to table below, and no eAxC links are involved, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_23429\_replaced([11100910](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

12747006:
Precoding and antenna port mapping
For FDD and eCPRI 7-2a, DU shall support 8-port CSI-RS transmission with configuration row 6 (TS 38.211 Table 7.4.1.5.3-1):

For X in {0 … 3}, each port of the pair (3000+X, 3004+X) is sending on separate polarization of subpanel X.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid representing resource allocation for CSI-RS signals. The grid has 12 columns labeled "slot" and an unspecified number of rows labeled "RB" (Resource Block). The grid is divided into smaller cells, each representing a unit of resource.
On the right side of the grid, there are four colored blocks indicating specific CSI-RS signal allocations:
1. **CDM#0 (Green):** Occupies the bottom-rightmost cells. It is associated with antenna ports P0 and P1, and has a polarization of +45.
2. **CDM#1 (Green):** Occupies the cells directly above CDM#0. It is associated with antenna ports P2 and P3, and has a polarization of +45.
3. **CDM#2 (Light Blue):** Occupies cells in the top-right section of the grid. It is associated with antenna ports P4 and P5, and has a polarization of -45.
4. **CDM#3 (Light Blue):** Occupies the cells directly above CDM#2. It is associated with antenna ports P6 and P7, and has a polarization of -45.
The figure caption states: "Figure: Example allocation of CSI-RS signal for row 6 – CDM2 (FD:2) with mapping of the antenna ports."
The grid structure suggests a time-frequency plane, where columns represent time slots and rows represent resource blocks. The colored blocks highlight specific configurations of CSI-RS signals within this resource grid, detailing the associated antenna ports and polarization.
The table below summarizes the information from the colored blocks:

| CDM ID | Antenna Ports | Polarization | Color |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CDM#0 | P0, P1 | +45 | Green |
| CDM#1 | P2, P3 | +45 | Green |
| CDM#2 | P4, P5 | -45 | Light Blue |
| CDM#3 | P6, P7 | -45 | Light Blue |

DU shall send each CSI-RS symbol on 4 fronthaul streams mapped to 2 polarizations accordingly:

1. stream: ports {3000, 3002} on polarization +45
2. stream: ports {3001, 3003} on polarization +45
3. stream: ports {3004, 3006} on polarization -45
4. stream: ports {3005, 3007} on polarization -45

L1 HWs supporting this configuration are listed in [11100474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knNSvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more explanations about mapping to polarization.

Figures location: [CSI-RS\_re\_allocation\_and\_port\_mapping.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/DL%20-%20CSI-RS%20Transmission/CSI-RS_re_allocation_and_port_mapping.pptx?d=wd11b9b5ca0394ef6a63d37c0c3cc8390&csf=1&web=1&e=667Ame)

###### 2.3.4.2.1.1.0-10 Precoding and antenna port mapping 32 port CSI-RS :  antenna port mapping for TDD**** (ID: 11100366)

**2.3.4.2.1.1.0-10.0-2**  (ID: `11100373`)

Precoding and antenna port mapping
**[Before CB008322-A]For TDD FR1, 32 port CSI-RS is transmitted on pair polarizations as below,**

* ports 3000-3015 shall be sent on polarization +45
* ports 3016-3031 shall be sent on polarization -45
* Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more explanations about mapping to polarization.

For X in {0 … 15}, each port of the pair (3000+X, 3016+X) is sent on separate polarization of subpanel X.

**[End CB008322-A]**

**[CB008322-A]Note: this requirement is not supported from E2E perspective. But, SW codes for this requirement still need to be inherited with deactivation** [End CB008322-A]\*\*\*\*

[Before CB008322-A] For TDD FR1 and eCPRI case, 32 ports CSI-RS are supported, CSI-RS ports mapping to eAxC streams as below table.
 
| | | |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CSI-RS port | eAxC | Polarization |
| 3000, 3008 | 0 | +45 |
| 3001, 3009 | 2 | +45 |
| 3016, 3024 | 1 | -45 |
| 3017, 3025 | 3 | -45 |
 
Please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more explanations about mapping to polarization.
 
For CSI-RS configuration row18 in Table 7.4.1.5.3-1 of 38.211:
For 1st symbol, [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a black background. The equation reads: S₀₊₈ⱼ = S₂₊₈ⱼ = S₄₊₈ⱼ = S₆₊₈ⱼ.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] , [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a black background. The equation reads: S₁₊₈ⱼ = S₃₊₈ⱼ = S₅₊₈ⱼ = S₇₊₈ⱼ.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]
For 2nd symbol, [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a black background. The equation is:
$S\_{0+8j} = -S\_{2+8j} = S\_{4+8j} = -S\_{6+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.003.png[/image\_path][/image-2] , [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a light background. The equation is:
$S\_{1+8i} = -S\_{3+8i} = S\_{5+8i} = -S\_{7+8i}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.004.png[/image\_path][/image-3]
For 3rd symbol, [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a white background. The equation is:
$S\_{0+8j} = S\_{2+8j} = -S\_{4+8j} = -S\_{6+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.005.png[/image\_path][/image-4] , [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a light background. The equation is:
$S\_1 + 8j = S\_3 + 8j = -S\_5 + 8j = -S\_7 + 8j$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.006.png[/image\_path][/image-5]
For 4th symbol, [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a white background. The equation is:
$S\_{0+8j} = -S\_{2+8j} = -S\_{4+8j} = S\_{6+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.007.png[/image\_path][/image-6] , [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a black background. The equation is:
$S\_{1+8j} = -S\_{3+8j} = -S\_{5+8j} = S\_{7+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.008.png[/image\_path][/image-7]
where [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains the letters "Sp" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow, giving them a metallic or iridescent appearance. The background is white, and the letters are positioned in the lower left corner of the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1343.009.png[/image\_path][/image-8] is the signal of CSI-RS port p, and j is the CDM ground index. So L1 only needs to transmit the signal of CSI-RS port 0, 1, 8, 9, 16, 17, 24, 25. RU shall transmit all of the CSI-RS ports according to above formula.
 
And for CSI-RS sequence generation, port0 is same to port8 except RE positions are different. Similar for port1 and port9, port16 and port24, port17 and port25. With FD beamforming, we can then combine CSI port0 with port8, port1 with port9, port 16 with port24 and port17 with port25, then we can finally use 4 streams for CSI-RS by combine ports with same sequence. In the eCPRI fast control packets(FCP), we need to have 2 sections for one stream.
For example, if k0=0 and K1=2 for CSI-RS row18, then we have one section for port0 with reMask=110000000000 with beam1 and another section for port8 with reMask=001100000000 with beam2. These 2 sections have same PRB range and will be mapped to same UP packet of same section ID.
 
More details can be found from below link:
[End CB008322-A]
[CB008322-A]Note: this requirement is not supported from E2E perspective. But, SW codes for this requirement still need to be inherited with deactivation [End CB008322-A]

---

Precoding and antenna port mapping

Precoding and antenna port mapping
For FDD FR1 and eCPRI 7-2a, DU shall support 32-port CSI-RS transmission with configuration row18 (TS 38.211 Table 7.4.1.5.3-1):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the mapping of 32 CS-RS ports to 32 TRXs. The diagram is organized into two main rows, each representing a different set of ports. Each row is further divided into two sections by a vertical purple line.
The top row shows ports labeled P1, P3, P5, P7 on the left side of the purple line, and P9, P11, P13, P15 on the right side. Below these, ports P17, P19, P21, P23 are shown on the left, and P25, P27, P29, P31 on the right. Each port is represented by two intersecting lines, one red and one blue, forming an 'X' shape. Above the top row, there are labels "+45" and "-45", indicating two levels. The ports P1, P3, P5, P7, P9, P11, P13, P15 are associated with the "+45" level, while P17, P19, P21, P23, P25, P27, P29, P31 are associated with the "-45" level. Arrows are present below the "-45" level ports, indicating a direction or connection.
The bottom row shows ports labeled P0, P2, P4, P6 on the left side of the purple line, and P8, P10, P12, P14 on the right side. Below these, ports P16, P18, P20, P22 are shown on the left, and P24, P26, P28, P30 on the right. Similar to the top row, each port is represented by intersecting red and blue lines. The "+45" and "-45" labels are also present for this row. Ports P0, P2, P4, P6, P8, P10, P12, P14 are associated with the "+45" level, and P16, P18, P20, P22, P24, P26, P28, P30 are associated with the "-45" level. Arrows are also present below the "-45" level ports in this row.
The text at the bottom of the image reads: "Figure: Mapping of 32 CS-RS ports to 32 TRXs".
The diagram visually represents a complex interconnection or mapping scheme, likely for telecommunications or networking equipment, where ports are assigned to specific transceivers (TRXs) based on their labels and the indicated connections. The use of red and blue lines and the 'X' shape suggests a cross-connection or a specific type of signal routing. The arrows might indicate the direction of data flow or a specific configuration.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-09081426.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

For X in {0 … 15}, each port of the pair (3000+X, 3016+X) is sending on separate polarization of subpanel X.

[image-1][image\_desc]The image displays a grid representing a time-frequency resource allocation for CSI-RS signals. The vertical axis is labeled "RB" (Resource Block), and the horizontal axis represents a "slot". The grid is divided into smaller cells, each representing a subcarrier in a Resource Block over time.
On the right side of the grid, four distinct colored blocks are shown, each representing a "CDM Group" (Code Division Multiplexing Group) with associated antenna ports and polarization.
- **CDM Group 3:** Occupies the top right section, colored light blue. It covers antenna ports P24 to P31 and has a polarization of -45.
- **CDM Group 2:** Located below CDM Group 3, colored blue. It covers antenna ports P16 to P23 and has a polarization of -45.
- **CDM Group 1:** Situated below CDM Group 2, colored light green. It covers antenna ports P8 to P15 and has a polarization of +45.
- **CDM Group 0:** Occupies the bottom right section, colored green. It covers antenna ports P0 to P7 and has a polarization of +45.
The text below the grid provides a caption: "Figure: Example allocation of CSI-RS signal for row 18 – CDM8 (FD:2, TD:4) with mapping of the antenna ports." This indicates that the diagram illustrates a specific CSI-RS configuration with Frequency Domain (FD) and Time Domain (TD) parameters, and how antenna ports are mapped within this allocation.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-10031759.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

DU shall send each CSI-RS symbol on 4 fronthaul streams mapped to 2 polarizations accordingly:

1. stream: ports {3000, 3008} on polarization +45
2. stream: ports {3001, 3009} on polarization +45
3. stream: ports {3016, 3024} on polarization -45
4. stream: ports {3017, 3025} on polarization -45

Note:

For CSI-RS configuration row18 in Table 7.4.1.5.3-1 of 38.211:

For 1st symbol, [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a black background. The equation is:
$S\_{0+8j} = S\_{2+8j} = S\_{4+8j} = S\_{6+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.001.png[/image\_path][/image-2] , [image-3][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a black background. The equation is:
$s\_{1+8j} = s\_{3+8j} = s\_{5+8j} = s\_{7+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.002.png[/image\_path][/image-3]

For 2nd symbol, [image-4][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a black background. The equation is:
$S\_{0+8j} = -S\_{2+8j} = S\_{4+8j} = -S\_{6+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.003.png[/image\_path][/image-4] , [image-5][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a light background. The equation is:
$S\_{1+8i} = -S\_{3+8i} = S\_{5+8i} = -S\_{7+8i}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.004.png[/image\_path][/image-5]

For 3rd symbol, [image-6][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in a blurry font. The equation is:
$S\_{0+8j} = S\_{2+8j} = -S\_{4+8j} = -S\_{6+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.005.png[/image\_path][/image-6] , [image-7][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a light background. The equation is:
$s\_1 + 8j = s\_3 + 8j = -s\_5 + 8j = -s\_7 + 8j$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.006.png[/image\_path][/image-7]

For 4th symbol, [image-8][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a white background. The equation is:
$S\_{0+8j} = -S\_{2+8j} = -S\_{4+8j} = S\_{6+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.007.png[/image\_path][/image-8] , [image-9][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a white background. The equation is:
$S\_{1+8j} = -S\_{3+8j} = -S\_{5+8j} = S\_{7+8j}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.008.png[/image\_path][/image-9]

where [image-10][image\_desc]The image contains the letters "Sp" in a stylized font. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow, giving them a metallic or iridescent appearance. The background is white, and the letters are positioned in the lower left corner of the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1342.009.png[/image\_path][/image-10] is the signal of CSI-RS port p, and j is the CDM group index. DU only needs to transmit the signal of CSI-RS port 0, 1, 8, 9, 16, 17, 24, 25. Radio shall transmit all of the CSI-RS ports according to above formula.

For CSI-RS sequence generation, port0 is same to port8 except RE positions are different. Similar for port1 and port9, port16 and port24, port17 and port25. With beamforming, we can then combine CSI-RS port0 with port8, port1 with port9, port 16 with port24 and port17 with port25, then we can finally use 4 streams for CSI-RS by combining ports with the same sequence. In the eCPRI fast control packets(FCP), we need to have 2 sections for one stream.

For example, if k0=0 and k1=2 for CSI-RS row18, then we have one section for port0 with reMask=110000000000 with beam1 and another section for port8 with reMask=001100000000 with beam2. These 2 sections have same PRB range and will be mapped to same UP packet of same section ID.

L1 HWs supporting this configuration are listed in [11100474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knNSvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Please refer to:

* [11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for more explanations about mapping to polarization.
* [11379137](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uee0q_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) for information regarding reMask

Figures location: [CSI-RS\_re\_allocation\_and\_port\_mapping.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/DL%20-%20CSI-RS%20Transmission/CSI-RS_re_allocation_and_port_mapping.pptx?d=wd11b9b5ca0394ef6a63d37c0c3cc8390&csf=1&web=1&e=667Ame)

Precoding and antenna port mapping
For FDD FR1 and eCPRI 7-2a, DU shall support 32-port CSI-RS transmission with configuration row16 (TS 38.211 Table 7.4.1.5.3-1):

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a grid representing CSI-RS signal allocation for row 16, specifically CDM2 (FD:2), with mapping of antenna ports. The grid is composed of cells, and some cells are colored green or blue, indicating allocated CSI-RS resources. Each colored cell contains text detailing the CDM number, antenna port assignments (e.g., P6, P7), and polarization (Pol:+45 or Pol:-45).
The text at the bottom of the image reads: "Figure: Example allocation of CSI-RS signal for row 16 – CDM2 (FD:2) with mapping of the antenna ports."
The colored cells are arranged in columns, with each column representing a different CDM number. The text within these cells is as follows:
**Column 1 (Green):**
\* **CDM#3:** P6, P7, Pol:+45
\* **CDM#2:** P4, P5, Pol:+45
\* **CDM#1:** P2, P3, Pol:+45
\* **CDM#0:** P0, P1, Pol:+45
**Column 2 (Green):**
\* **CDM#7:** P14, P15, Pol:+45
\* **CDM#6:** P12, P13, Pol:+45
\* **CDM#5:** P10, P11, Pol:+45
\* **CDM#4:** P8, P9, Pol:+45
**Column 3 (Blue):**
\* **CDM#11:** P22, P23, Pol:-45
\* **CDM#10:** P20, P21, Pol:-45
\* **CDM#9:** P18, P19, Pol:-45
\* **CDM#8:** P16, P17, Pol:-45
**Column 4 (Blue):**
\* **CDM#15:** P30, P31, Pol:-45
\* **CDM#14:** P28, P29, Pol:-45
\* **CDM#13:** P26, P27, Pol:-45
\* **CDM#12:** P24, P25, Pol:-45
The grid itself appears to be 8 rows by 12 columns, with the colored cells occupying the rightmost 4 columns and the bottom 4 rows. The remaining cells are uncolored and appear to be empty or unallocated.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-11031253.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

DU shall send each CSI-RS symbol on 2 fronthaul streams mapped to 1 polarization at a time:

at symbol l0 :

1. stream: ports {3000, 3002, 3004, 3006} on polarization +45
2. stream: ports {3001, 3003, 3005, 3007} on polarization +45

at symbol l0 +1:

1. stream: ports {3008, 3010, 3012, 3014} on polarization +45
2. stream: ports {3009, 3011, 3013, 3015} on polarization +45

at symbol l1 :

1. stream: ports {3016, 3018, 3020, 3022} on polarization -45
2. stream: ports {3017, 3019, 3021, 3023} on polarization -45

at symbol l1 +1:

1. stream: ports {3024, 3026, 3028, 3030} on polarization -45
2. stream: ports {3025, 3027, 3029, 3031} on polarization -45

L1 HWs supporting this configuration are listed in [11100474](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knNSvPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

Please refer to:

* [11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) for more explanations about mapping to polarization.

Figures location: [CSI-RS\_re\_allocation\_and\_port\_mapping.pptx](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20UP%20Algorithms/2%20L1%20Algorithms%20and%20Configurations/DL%20-%20CSI-RS%20Transmission/CSI-RS_re_allocation_and_port_mapping.pptx?d=wd11b9b5ca0394ef6a63d37c0c3cc8390&csf=1&web=1&e=667Ame)

##### 2.3.4.2.1.2 Supported Configurations

**2.3.4.2.1.2.0-1.0-9**  (ID: `13464558`)

Supported Configurations

| CSI-RS ports | Loner L1 HW | Loki L1 HW | Thor L1 HW | Thor L1 HW (Radio 7-2e) | Odin | GPU |
| 2 ports ([11100249](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMElvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | supported | supported | **supported** | supported | - | supported |
| 4 ports (Identity precoding) ([11100259](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMEmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | supported | supported | **supported** | supported | supported | supported |
| 4 ports (diagonal precoding) ([11100275](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMEnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | supported | supported | **supported** | supported | supported | supported |
| 8 ports ([11100312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | supported | supported | **supported** | **supported** | - | supported |
| 8 ports (SSB beam based cell specific CSI-RS precoding) ([11100290](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMEn_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | - | supported | **supported** | **supported** | - | - |
| 32 ports ([11100366](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrq_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | ***\*-***\* | **-** | ***\*-***\* | ***\*-***\* | **-** | **-** |
| **First feature** |  |  | CB007487-B CB007487-C | CB009170-D | CB014420-HA | CB016066-C |

#### 2.3.4.2.2 CSI-RS for tracking : precoding and antenna port mapping

##### 2.3.4.2.2.1 1 CSI-RS port - 1x1 DL MIMO mode

###### 2.3.4.2.2.1.0-1 1 CSI-RS port - 1x1 DL MIMO mode Loki and Thor L1 HW (ID: 11100499)

**2.3.4.2.2.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11100507`)

1 CSI-RS port - 1x1 DL MIMO mode
For  FR1 FDD Loki [CB009029-5G-A] and Thor [End CB009029-5G-A] L1 HW, the mapping described here shall be applied for the below gNB configurations (similar as for SSB):

* configuration for 1x1 DL MIMO ( NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = 1x1 Single Antenna transmission)

The mapping of the logical antenna port for CSI-RS for tracking signal to polarizations shall be done as given in below table.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| rdSyncSignalsTxMode (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8454\_replaced([11110258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for tracking | Polarization |
| 0 | 3000 | polarization +45° |

---

##### 2.3.4.2.2.2 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 2x2 DL MIMO mode and beamforming

###### 2.3.4.2.2.2.0-1 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 2x2 DL MIMO mode and beamforming Loner, Loki, Thor, Odin and GPU L1 HWs (ID: 11100513)

**2.3.4.2.2.2.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12768181`)

1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 2x2 DL MIMO mode and beamforming
DU (for TDD FR1/FR2/FDD Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HWs), [CB016066-C] DU (for TDD FR1 GPU L1HW)[End CB016066-C] and Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e TDD FR1 Thor L1HW) shall apply the mapping described here for the below gNB configurations (similar as for SSB):

* Beamforming configuration, regardless the DL MIMO mode (see [11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), enabled by:

- MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = true, [CB013442-D] for TDD [CB008577-A] and FDD [End CB008577-A],

- flag rdFddBfL3Call (see [11329923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TtGWePvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) = true, for FDD. [End CB013442-D]

Note, FDD only support BF for Thor L1 HW.

* Non beamforming configuration for 2x2 DL MIMO (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = 2x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing or 2x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing)

The mapping of the logical antenna port for CSI-RS for tracking signals to polarizations are described in below table.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| *rdSyncSignalsTxMode* ([11110258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for tracking | Polarization |
| 0 | 3000 | polarization +45° |
| 1 | 3000 | polarization -45° |
| 2 | **3000** | polarizations +/-45° |

If *rdSyncSignalsTxMode is set to 2, DU and Radio shall apply the multiplexing to CSI-RS for tracking using this precoding vector [1 1]. This multiplexing operation is described in the section 3.3 of 5GMax algorithm specification document (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

[CB016066-C] From E2E perspective, DU (for GPU L1HW) shall support only the configuration corresponding to rdSyncSignalsTxMode equals to 2. Note: Due to L1 SW limitation, rdSyncSignalsTxMode is not supported for GPU L1 HW.  
DU (for GPU L1HW) and [End CB016066-C] Radio (for Thor 7-2e L1HW) shall support Beamforming configuration only (i.e. Non-Beamforming configurations not supported).

---

###### 2.3.4.2.2.2.0-2 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 2x2 DL MIMO mode and beamforming L1 vDU HW (ID: 11100532)

**2.3.4.2.2.2.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11100539`)

1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 2x2 DL MIMO mode and beamforming
[Before CNI-103796-M]

For FDD FR1 and TDD FR1 vDU L1 HW, and for below gNB configurations:

* Non beamforming configuration for 2x2 DL MIMO (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = FALSE and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = 2x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing or 2x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing)

And for TDD FR1 vDU L1 HW, and for below gNB configurations:

* Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = TRUE) regardless the DL MIMO mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

CSI-RS for tracking logical antenna port is always mapped to the polarizations described in below table using the precoding vector in the last column. (similar as for SSB)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for tracking | Polarization | Precoding Vector |
| 3000 | polarizations +/-45° | [1 1] |

[End CNI-103796-M]

---

##### 2.3.4.2.2.3 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode

###### 2.3.4.2.2.3.0-1 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode Loner and Loki L1 HWs (ID: 11100545)

**2.3.4.2.2.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11100552`)

1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode
[CB013440-A] This requirement is valid if NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = False or if NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode = [Before CB013440-A-UPD002] 2 [CB013440-A-UPD002] 5 [End CB013440-A-UPD002] (see 5G\_UP\_13733\_replaced([11353784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucB9oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), [End CB013440-A]

{

For Loner and Loki, FDD and TDD FR1, DU shall apply the mapping described here for the below gNB configurations (same as for SSB):

* Non Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = FALSE) and 4x4 or 4x2 DL MIMO (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = '4x4 or 4x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing' or '4x4 or 4x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing' (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The mapping of the logical antenna port for CSI-RS for tracking to polarizations shall be done as given in below table using the precdoing vector in the last column. More details about the multiplexing opertaion is described in the section 3.3 of 5GMax algorithm specification document (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| rdSyncSignalsTxMode (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8454\_replaced([11110258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for tracking | Polarization | Precoding Vector |
| 0 | 3000 | +45° of X-Pol1 +45° of X-Pol2 | [1 0 1 0] |
| 1 | 3000 | -45° of X-Pol1 -45° of X-Pol2 | [0 1 0 1] |
| 2 | 3000 | `+45°/-45° of X-Pol1 +45°/-45° of X-Pol2 | [1 1 1 1] |

}

###### 2.3.4.2.2.3.0-2 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode vDU L1 HW (ID: 11100556)

**2.3.4.2.2.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11100564`)

1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode
[Before CNI-103796-M]

For FDD FR1 and TDD FR1 vDU L1 HW, and for below gNB configurations:

* Non Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = FALSE) and 4x4 or 4x2 DL MIMO (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = '4x4 or 4x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing' or '4x4 or 4x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing' (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

CSI-RS for tracking logical antenna port is always mapped to the polarizations described in below table using the precoding vector in the last column.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for tracking | Polarization | Precoding Vector |
| 3000 | `+45°/-45° of X-Pol1 +45°/-45° of X-Pol2 | [1 1 1 1] |

[End CNI-103796-M]

###### 2.3.4.2.2.3.0-3 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode Thor and GPU L1 HWs (ID: 11100568)

**2.3.4.2.2.3.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `13435548`)

1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode
This requirement is if *NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = False or if* NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode =**5 (see [11353784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucB9oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),**\*\*

{

For Thor(FDD and TDD FR1), [CB016406-A]GPU(FDD)[End CB016406-A], DU shall apply the mapping described here for the below gNB configurations (same as for SSB):

* Non Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = FALSE) and 4x4 or 4x2 DL MIMO (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = '4x4 or 4x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing' or '4x4 or 4x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing' (see [11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The mapping of the logical antenna port for CSI-RS for tracking to polarizations shall be done as given in below table using the precoding vector in the last column. More details about the multiplexing operation is described in the section 3.3 of 5GMax algorithm specification document (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| *rdSyncSignalsTxMode* (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8454\_replaced([11110258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for tracking | Polarization | Precoding Vector | **First Feature** |
| 0 | 3000 | +45° of X-Pol1 +45° of X-Pol2 | [1 0 1 0] | CB008325-5G-AA CB008325-5G-BA |
| 1 | 3000 | -45° of X-Pol1 -45° of X-Pol2 | [0 1 0 1] | CB008325-5G-AA CB008325-5G-BA |
| 2 | 3000 | `+45°/-45° of X-Pol1 +45°/-45° of X-Pol2 | [1 1 1 1] | CB007487- B CB007487-D |

[CB016406-A]From E2E perspective, DU (for GPU L1HW) shall support only the configuration corresponding to rdSyncSignalsTxMode equals to 2. Note: Due to L1 SW limitation, rdSyncSignalsTxMode is not supported for GPU L1 HW.[End CB016406-A]

}

###### 2.3.4.2.2.3.0-4 1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode Loner, Loki and Thor L1 HWs when NIDD parameter NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans is activated**** (ID: 11100582)

**2.3.4.2.2.3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13345268`)

1 CSI-RS port - Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode
**Purpose:**

Enhance TRS (or CSI-RS for tracking) precoding scheme when NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = True and (NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode = 4 or 6 (see [11353784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucB9oPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**Inputs:**

* NIDD parameter: *MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming*
* NIDD parameter: *NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode*
* NIDD parameter: *MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actEnhCtrlChannelTrans*
* NIDD parameter: *NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode*
* TRS enhancement activation, see [11159870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktMl9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**Algorithm**

For Non-Beamforming (i.e. *NRCELL/actBeamforming = FALSE) and 4x4 or 4x2 DL MIMO (i.e.* NRCELL/dlMimoMode = '4x4 or 4x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing' or '4x4 or 4x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing' (see [11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and actEnhCtrlChannelTrans set to TRUE:\*\*

When TRS enhancement is activated (see [11159870](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktMl9PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), for [CB016028-A]Loner,[End CB016028-A] Loki and Thor L1 HWs , DU shall apply, starting the current TRS slot, the mapping of the logical antenna ports CSI-RS for tracking to polarizations as given in below table using the precoding vector in the last column. More details about the multiplexing operation is described in the section 3.3 of 5GMax algorithm specification document (see [11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **NIDD parameter configuration (\*)** | **Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for tracking (or TRS) ([11094921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmhWMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))** | **Polarization** | **Precoding Vector** |
| *NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = True and* *NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode = 4 or 6* | 3000 | +45°/-45° of X-Pol1 | [1 1 0 0] |

[CB016028-A](*) DU (for Loner L1 HW) shall support *NRCELL.actEnhCtrlChannelTrans = True only for FDD with*NRCELL.enhCtrlChannelTransMode = 4 [End CB016028-A]\*\*

Otherwise (when TRS enhancement is not activated), for [CB016028-A]Loner,[End CB016028-A] Loki and Thor L1 HWs, DU  shall use the precoding vector and the mapping for CSI-RS for tracking as specified in [11100545](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knOg0vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) or [11100568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knOg2fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

Notes:

* Convention for 4Tx X-Pol cabling is: X-Pol1 +45, X-Pol1 -45, Xpol2 +45, XPol2 -45.
* Convention of requirements [11095739](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmoD6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11095747](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmoD7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) is: X-Pol1 +45, X-Pol2 +45, Xpol1 -45, XPol2 -45 which is different from above.

**Rationale:**

Please refer to [11353772](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucB9nfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)

**Outputs:**

Pre-coded TRS signals and mapped to the expected polarization.

---

#### 2.3.4.2.3 CSI-RS for refinement: precoding and antenna port mapping

##### 2.3.4.2.3.1 1 CSI-RS port - beamforming

###### 2.3.4.2.3.1.0-1 1 CSI-RS port - beamforming Loner and Loki L1 HWs (ID: 11100596)

**2.3.4.2.3.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11100599`)

1 CSI-RS port - beamforming
For TDD FR1 Loner and Loki L1 HWs, the mapping described here shall be applied for the below gNB configurations (similar as for SSB):

* Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = TRUE) regardless the DL MIMO mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The mapping of the logical antenna ports for CSI-RS for refinement signals to polarizations shall be done as given in below table.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| rdSyncSignalsTxMode (5G\_UP\_ALG\_8454\_replaced([11110258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for refinement | Polarization |
| 0 | 3000 | polarization +45° |
| 1 | 3000 | polarization -45° |
| 2 | 3000 | polarizations +/-45° |

If rdSyncSignalsTxMode is set to 2, multiplexing should be applied to CSI-RS for tracking using this precoding vector [1 1]. This multiplexing operation is described in the section 3.3 of 5GMax algorithm specification document (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_695\_replaced([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

---

###### 2.3.4.2.3.1.0-2 1 CSI-RS port - beamforming vDU L1 HW (ID: 11100603)

**2.3.4.2.3.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11100610`)

1 CSI-RS port - beamforming
[Before CNI-103796-M]

For TDD FR1 vDU L1 HW, and for below gNB configurations:

* Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = TRUE) regardless the DL MIMO mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

CSI-RS for refinement logical antenna port is always mapped to the polarizations described in below table using the precoding vector in the last column. (similar as for SSB)

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for refinement | Polarization | Precoding Vector |
| 3000 | polarizations +/-45° | [1 1] |

[End CNI-103796-M]

---

###### 2.3.4.2.3.1.0-3 1 CSI-RS port - beamforming Thor and GPU (ID: 11100614)

**2.3.4.2.3.1.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `13344820`)

1 CSI-RS port - beamforming
DU (for TDD FR1 Thor [CB016066-C] and GPU [End CB016066-C] L1HWs)  shall support:

* Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = TRUE) regardless the DL MIMO mode (see [11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

DU (for TDD FR1 Thor L1HW) shall support the mapping of the logical antenna ports for CSI-RS for refinement signals to polarizations as given in below table (similar as for SSB).

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| *rdSyncSignalsTxMode* ([11110258](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpB3qPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | Logical antenna port(s) for CSI-RS for refinement | Polarization | **First Feature** |
| 0 | 3000 | polarization +45° | CB008325-5G-CA |
| 1 | 3000 | polarization -45° | CB008325-5G-CA |
| 2 | 3000 | polarizations +/-45° | CB007487- C |

If *rdSyncSignalsTxMode is set to 2, multiplexing should be applied to CSI-RS for tracking using this precoding vector [1 1]. This multiplexing operation is described in the section 3.3 of 5GMax algorithm specification document (see ([11101310](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcovvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).*

[CB016066-C] From E2E perspective, DU (for GPU L1HW) shall support only the configuration corresponding to rdSyncSignalsTxMode equals to 2. [End CB016066-C]

---

### 2.3.4.3 CSI-RS rate matching

#### 2.3.4.3.1 NZP CSI-RS rate matching

##### 2.3.4.3.1.0-1 NZP CSI-RS rate matching Overview (ID: 11100630)

**2.3.4.3.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11100635`)

NZP CSI-RS rate matching
DU [CB009514-J] or Radio(eCPRI 7-2e)[End CB009514-J] shall support NZP CSI-RS rate matching  in SSB and TRS slot to support PDSCH transmission in SSB and TRS slots. This is supported for both FR1 TDD and FR1 FDD.

[CB009514-J]

Note:

* Added link to 5G-L1-DL(Radio) for eCPRI 7-2e. Note shall be removed in next version.
* FDD is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

[End CB009514-J]

---

##### 2.3.4.3.1.0-2 NZP CSI-RS rate matching NZP CSI-RS rate matching in TDD FR1 (ID: 11100640)

**2.3.4.3.1.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11100654`)

NZP CSI-RS rate matching
In BF (non-FD BF) case, in the slots where are scheduled the CSI-RS for tracking or SSB slots, all the DL channels or signals shall be handled through more than one C4 subcells for one component carrier (for details see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_518\_replaced([11099011](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knBsoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In CSI-RS for tracking slots, the CSI-RS for tracking shall be handled by one C4 subcell. In case of CSI-RS FDM enabled, these CSI-RS resources are transmitted on different spatial streams (two spatial streams per CSI-RS resource). If no CSI-RS for tracking FDM, 1 PDSCH with RANK2 can be transmitted in this subcell, but use the two streams different than TRS (note that, for TRS stream index, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_3583\_replaced([11159730](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktLX3fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In FD BF case, DU or Radio (eCPRI 7-2e) can handle CSI-RS for tracking and PDSCH in one C4 subcell or more than one C4 subcells. Note that, in one C4 subcell, DU or Radio (eCPRI 7-2e) shall transmit the different CSI-RS for tracking resources by different eAxC links, i.e., two eAxC per CSI-RS resource, one C4 subcell supports two CSI-RS for tracking resource.

In non beamforming case, DU shall support PDSCH transmission in SSB and TRS slots without special handling, because there is only one sector beam used. PDSCH, SSB and TRS can be handled by one subcell.

When NRBTS.NRCELL.actSubTrsRateMatch is TRUE, DU shall support PDSCH rate matching in TRS slots with TRS symbol granularity level, i.e. PDSCH RE not mapped on TRS symbols. (Note: gNB-DU shall configure ZP-CSI-RS-Resource(row 9 in Table 7.4.1.5.3-1 of 38.211) at RRC level to exclude all REs on TRS symbols in UE side. The detailed configuration refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_13244\_replaced([11302024](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tqd5MPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). About RE mapping on PDSCH and CSI-RS for tracking, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25599\_replaced([11099399](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knEIwvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). )

Note:

* Non beamforming and Non FD BF are not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

[Before CB009936-F]

* Added link to 5G-L1-DL(Radio) for eCPRI 7-2e. Note shall be removed in next version.
* PDSCH rate matching in TRS slots with TRS symbol granularity level is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

[End CB009936-F]

---

##### 2.3.4.3.1.0-3 NZP CSI-RS rate matching NZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD FR1 (ID: 11100657)

**2.3.4.3.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11100660`)

NZP CSI-RS rate matching
For NZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD FR1, no special handling is needed to support PDSCH transmission in SSB and TRS slots because there is only one sectore beam used. PDSCH, SSB and TRS can be handled by one subcell.

---

##### 2.3.4.3.1.0-4 NZP CSI-RS rate matching Supported NZP CSI-RS rate matching in TDD FR1 (ID: 11100663)

**2.3.4.3.1.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `11100682`)

NZP CSI-RS rate matching
DU or Radio(eCPRI 7-2e) shall support NZP CSI-RS rate matching in TDD FR1 for different L1 variants as below table:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | NZP CSI-RS rate matching in TDD FR1 (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_673\_replaced([11100640](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knPH7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | NZP CSI-RS rate matching in TDD FR1, for PDSCH rate matching in TRS slots with TRS symbol granularity level (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_673\_replaced([11100640](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knPH7fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | First feature |
| Loner | supported | - |  |
| Loki | supported | supported |  |
| Thor | supported | supported | CB008325-5G-AA |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | supported | [CB009936-F] supported[End CB009936-F] | CB009514-J |

---

##### 2.3.4.3.1.0-5 NZP CSI-RS rate matching Supported NZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD FR1 (ID: 11100684)

**2.3.4.3.1.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11100692`)

NZP CSI-RS rate matching
The supported NZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD FR1 for different L1 variants is as below table:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | NZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD FR1 (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_675\_replaced([11100657](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knPu8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | First feature |
| Loner | supported |  |
| Loki | supported |  |
| vDU | - |  |
| Thor | supported | CB008325-5G-BA |

---

#### 2.3.4.3.2 ZP CSI-RS rate matching

##### 2.3.4.3.2.0-1 ZP CSI-RS rate matching Overview (ID: 11100698)

**2.3.4.3.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11100709`)

ZP CSI-RS rate matching
For FR1 FDD and FR1 TDD, DU [CB009936-F]  and Radio (eCPRI 7-2e TDD FR1 only)[End CB009936-F] shall support ZP CSI-RS rate matching for CSI-IM (see 38.214 §5.2.2.4).

[CB009936-F]

Note:

* Added link to 5G-L1-DL(Radio) for eCPRI 7-2e. Note shall be removed in next version.
* FDD is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

[End CB009936-F]

---

##### 2.3.4.3.2.0-2 ZP CSI-RS rate matching ZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD DSS (for CSI-IM pattern 0) and in FDD non DSS (for CSI-IM pattern 1) (ID: 11100711)

**2.3.4.3.2.0-2.0-5**  (ID: `11100728`)

ZP CSI-RS rate matching
For FR1 FDD DSS only , CSI-IM can be scheduled in DL normal slots (not in SSB slots, nor TRS slots).

For FR1 FDD non DSS , CSI-IM can be scheduled in DL normal slots and **only for Loner** [CNI-142857] and for LOKI HW (when no BB pooling configured) **[End CNI-142857] in SSB slots (not in TRS slots)**.***\****\*

The REs which are CSI-RS rate matched with ZP CSI-RS don't transmit any signal on any PDSCH spatial streams. For further details on PDSCH rate matching wth CSI-IM, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_541\_replaced([11099450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knEIzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For A2 subcells, in higher layers, the CSI-IM pattern 0 or 1 is still mapped with ZP CSI-RS row 5 (for CSI-IM pattern 0) or ZP CSI-RS row 4 (for CSI-IM pattern 1) **but it can be seen like 2 adjacent ZP CSI-RS row 3.**

For A1 subcells, in higher layers, the CSI-IM pattern 0 or 1 is still mapped with ZP CSI-RS row 5 (for CSI-IM pattern 0) or ZP CSI-RS row 4 (for CSI-IM pattern 1) but it can be seen like 4 adjacent ZP CSI-RS row 2.

For supported L1 HW refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_687\_replaced([11100741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knQWDPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.3.4.3.2.0-3 ZP CSI-RS rate matching ZP CSI-RS rate matching in TDD FR1 (for CSI-IM pattern 1) (ID: 11100732)

**2.3.4.3.2.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11100737`)

ZP CSI-RS rate matching
For TDD FR1, DU [CB009936-F] or Radio (eCPRI 7-2e)[End CB009936-F] shall support CSI-IM pattern 1 in DL normal slots (not in SSB slots, nor TRS slots). The detailed configuration refer to 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_13256\_replaced([11302056](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tqd5NPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The REs which are CSI-RS rate matched with ZP CSI-RS don't transmit any signal on any PDSCH spatial streams. For further details on PDSCH rate matching wth CSI-IM, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_541\_replaced([11099450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knEIzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For C2 subcells, in higher layers, the CSI-IM pattern 1 is still mapped with ZP CSI-RS row 4 but it can be seen like 2 adjacent ZP CSI-RS row 3.

For supported L1 HW refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_687\_replaced([11100741](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knQWDPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB009936-F]

Note:

* C2 subcells not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

[End CB009936-F]

---

##### 2.3.4.3.2.0-4 ZP CSI-RS rate matching Supported ZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD FR1 and TDD FR1(for CSI-IM) (ID: 11100741)

**2.3.4.3.2.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `11100757`)

ZP CSI-RS rate matching
DU shall support ZP CSI-RS rate matching in FDD FR1 and DU [CB009936-F] or Radio (eCPRI 7-2e)[End CB009936-F]TDD FR1 for different L1 variants as below table:

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| L1 HW | ZP CSI-RS rate matching for FR1 FDD (for CSI-IM) (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_685\_replaced([11100711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knQWA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | ZP CSI-RS rate matching for FR1 FDD (for CSI-IM) for A1 subcell Type (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_685\_replaced([11100711](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knQWA_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | ZP CSI-RS rate matching for FR1 TDD (for CSI-IM) (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_25540\_replaced([11100732](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knQWCfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | First feature |
| Loner | supported | - | - | CB007738-B |
| Loki | supported | supported | supported | CB007738-B |
| Thor | supported | supported | supported | CB008325-5G-BA |
| Thor (Radio 7-2e) | - | - | supported | CB009936-F |

### 2.3.4.4 CSI-RS power setting

#### 2.3.4.4.0-1 CSI-RS power setting Overview (ID: 11100764)

**2.3.4.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11100769`)

CSI-RS power setting
Input:

* csiTransmitPower computed in 5G-L2-PS per CSI-RS resource according to:
* CSI-RS for tracking, see the algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2575\_replaced([11118592](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDyavvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* CSI-RS for refinement, see the algorithm in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2576\_replaced([11118605](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDybfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* CSI-RS for feedback, see the algorithms in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2577\_replaced([11118612](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDyb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for CSI-RS UE specific and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2578\_replaced([11118639](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqEZdPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for CSI-RS cell specific

Algorithm:

* The DU [CB009170-D] or Radio(L1)[End CB009170-D]  shall determine the DL CSI-RS EPRE (see 38.214 chapter 4.1) according to the value of csiTransmitPower. For CSI-RS for feedback only, DU(L1) [CB009170-D] or Radio(L1)[End CB009170-D] shall consider power offset for the first beam and second beam.

Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_15508\_replaced([11100792](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knQ9G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for power offset for the first beam and second beam.

Output:

Power setting per RE for the different CSI-RS modes: tracking/refinement/channel acquisition

For the supported CSI-RS mode per L1 HW, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_554\_replaced([11099841](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knHzK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB009170-D]

Note:

* "L1" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

[End CB009170-D]

---

#### 2.3.4.4.0-3 CSI-RS power setting CSI-RS power offset for the first beam and second beam (ID: 11100792)

**2.3.4.4.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11100797`)

CSI-RS power setting
For NRBTS/NRCELL/actDlSrsBm = True, NRBTS/NRCELL/actCsirsBeamPowerBalance= True and NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode=40 (4x4 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing), for UEs configured 4 port UE specific CSI RS for channel measurement, only when CSI-RS precoding disable (refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_404\_replaced([11097591](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km5JrfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the DU [CB009514-F] and Radio (for 7-2e) [End CB009514-F] shall consider CSI power offset for the first beam and second beam as defined by 5G\_UP\_ALG\_2577\_replaced([11118612](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqDyb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

See 5G\_UP\_ALG\_160\_replaced([11095739](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmoD6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the beam index to eAxC index mapping.

[CB009514-F]

Note:

- Add link to 5G-L1-DL(Radio) for eCPRI 7-2e. This note shall be deleted in next version.

[End CB009514-F]

### 2.3.4.5 CSI-RS Short-term Beam Computer - Radio

| SVD Algorithm | SVD Overview | [CB009527-D] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28337\_replaced([11100867](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLMvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[End CB009527-D] |
| Eigen Vector calculation | [CB009527-D] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28339\_replaced([11100876](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLNPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB009527-D] |  |
| BWV normalization | [CB009527-D] 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28343\_replaced([11100881](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLNvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [End CB009527-D] |  |
| First Feature |  | CB009527-B |

#### 2.3.4.5.0-2 CSI-RS Short-term Beam Computer - Radio Inputs/Outputs/Algorithm (ID: 11100828)

**2.3.4.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11100834`)

CSI-RS Short-term Beam Computer - Radio
For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, here are the inputs/output/algorithm of Short-term Beam computer.

This beam computer is done per UE upon CSI-RS scheduling request from DU.

Inputs:

* From DU, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_21094\_replaced([11156669](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks2ns_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

- Buffer identifier.

- Position of middle PRB within selected SRS SB.

* From Radio in uplink,

- Raw channel estimation see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_26688\_replaced([11107613](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kohhWPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

* From Radio,

- Number of TRX NTRX ∈ {64 [CB009527-D], 32 [End CB009527-D]}.  NTRX is equal to M*N*P, where M=number of TRX rows, N = number of TRX columns and P = 2 is number of TRX Polarizations.

Algorithm overview:

* Radio shall derive a dedicated BF subband according to the position of middle PRB within selected SRS SB.
* Radio shall compute the BWVs on the dedicated BF SB for CSI-RS beam with [Before CB009527-D] MMSE algorithm [End CB009527-D] [CB009527-D] with the configured short-term BF method [End CB009527-D], refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27041\_replaced([11100837](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkKfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [CB009527-D] for MMSE (Minimum Mean Squared Error) algorithm and refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_28336\_replaced([11100864](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLMfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for SVD (Singular Value Decomposition) algorithm [End CB009527-D]. Note, MMSE is also called as RZF (Regularized Zero-Forcing) algorithm.
* Radio shall apply the BWVs for CSI-RS.

Output:

* CSI-RS data after beamforming.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27410\_replaced([11100818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall perform short-term BF for CSI-RS if CSI beam is requested from DU. The below figure shows functions of short-term BF for CSI-RS. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27039\_replaced([11100828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the input and ouput of the algorithm.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a block diagram illustrating a "Short-term beam computer (MMSE)". The diagram shows a process flow with three main blocks: "MMSE", "BWV W normalization", and "CSI-RS Beamforming".
An input labeled "HP srs X NTX" enters the "MMSE" block. An arrow from above points to the "MMSE" block, indicating an input described as "Middle PRB of selected SRS subband".
The output of the "MMSE" block is an arrow labeled "Wcsi(NTRX x 4)" which feeds into the "BWV W normalization" block.
The output of the "BWV W normalization" block is another arrow labeled "Wcsi(NTRX x 4)" which feeds into the "CSI-RS Beamforming" block.
The entire process is enclosed within a larger rounded rectangle, signifying the "Short-term beam computer (MMSE)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1463.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
 
Here are the main steps of short-term BF for CSI-RS:
Step#1. Radio shall derive a dedicated BF subband according to the position of middle PRB within selected SRS SB. Radio shall calculate Wcsi on the dedicated BF subband for CSI-RS, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27043\_replaced([11100849](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
Step#2. Radio shall normalize Wcsi, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27044\_replaced([11100858](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and then apply the beam weights to UE specific CSI-RS.
File: Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx

For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall perform BWVs calculation with MMSE algorithm for each UE per CSI-RS scheduling request from DU.

If DU triggers short-term BF for CSI-RS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27039\_replaced([11100828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), Radio shall feed the 4 ports channel estimation H of the dedicated BF subband for MMSE, acquires BWVs Wcsi., it would be applied for 4 ports UE specific CSI-RS beams.

Radio shall use the R&D parameter rdMmsePowerFactor (5G\_UP\_ALG\_19840\_replaced([11110396](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDs3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) as MMSE regularization term [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of what appears to be a stylized eye or a pair of eyes. The colors are muted, with shades of brown, blue, and white blending together. The overall impression is one of a soft, diffused light source behind the shapes. There is no discernible text or table in the image.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1465.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0], see MMSE weight calculation formula option2, refer to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.3 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_700\_replaced([11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27410\_replaced([11100818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.3.4.5.1.0-3 MMSE Algorithm BWV normalization (ID: 11100858)

**2.3.4.5.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11100860`)

MMSE Algorithm
For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall normalize Wcsi according to R&D parameter rdMmseNorm, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_19843\_replaced([11110379](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpDs1_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

if rdMmseNorm =0, Wcsi is normalized per layer-wise and per TRX-wise.

If rdMmseNorm =1, Wcsi is normalize per TRX-wise only.

Please refer to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.3.1 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_700\_replaced([11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for more details.

The normalization per TRX-wise secures each TRX doesn't exceed maximum transmit power.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27410\_replaced([11100818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---
