For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall perform short-term BF for CSI-RS if CSI beam is requested from DU. The below figure shows functions of short-term BF for CSI-RS. Refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27039\_replaced([11100828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the input and ouput of the algorithm.
[image-0][image\_desc]The image depicts a block diagram illustrating the process of a "Short-term beam computer (SVD)". The diagram shows a flow of data through three main stages within a larger encompassing box.
Input to the system comes from two sources:
1. An arrow labeled "HP\_srs x N\_TRX" points to the first block.
2. An arrow from above, labeled "Middle PRB of selected SRS subband", points to the first block as well.
The three sequential blocks are:
1. **Eigen-vector calculation**: This block receives the inputs mentioned above.
2. **BWV W normalization**: This block receives an output from the first block, indicated by an arrow labeled "Wcsi(N\_TRX x P\_srs)".
3. **CSI-RS Beamforming**: This block receives an output from the second block, indicated by an arrow labeled "Wcsi(N\_TRX x P\_srs)".
The overall process is contained within a rounded rectangle labeled "Short-term beam computer (SVD)".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1470.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]
Radio shall derive a dedicated BF subband according to the position of middle PRB within selected SRS subband, the corresponding channel estimate HPsrs x NTRX of the selected SRS subband shall be fed for SVD algorithm according to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.2. Below are the main steps of SVD algorithm:
 
Step#1. Calculate Eigen Vector based on raw SRS channel estimation HPsrs x NTRX, refer to section 5.1.2.2.1.1 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification.
Step#2. Normalize WcsiNTRX x Psrs per TRX-wise, refer to section 5.1.2.2.3 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification.
 
Note, for SVD algorithm, the Eigen Vector actually means the right-singular vector.
 
File: Short and Long-term Beam Management.pptx
Folder:

---

##### 2.3.4.5.2.0-2 SVD Algorithm Eigen Vector calculation (ID: 11100876)

**2.3.4.5.2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11100878`)

SVD Algorithm
For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall perform BWVs calculation with SVD algorithm for each UE per CSI-RS scheduling request from DU.

If DU triggers short-term BF for CSI-RS (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27039\_replaced([11100828](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), Radio shall perform SVD based on the SRS channel estimation HPsrs x NTRX, and calculate Psrs eigen vectors uNTRX x 1 according to the singular values and the left-singular vectors of HPsrs x NTRX, where the Psrs is equal to 4 SRS ports. Note, for SVD algorithm, the Eigen Vector actually means the right-singular vector.

After eigen vectors calculation, Radio shall use all the eigen vectors to derive the WcsiNTRX x Psrs.

Refer to section 5.1.2.2.1.1 of 5Gmax Massive MIMO Algorithm specification for more details.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27410\_replaced([11100818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.3.4.5.2.0-3 SVD Algorithm BWV normalization (ID: 11100881)

**2.3.4.5.2.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11100884`)

SVD Algorithm
For TDD FR1 eCPRI 7-2e, Radio shall normalize WcsiNTRX x Psrs per TRX-wise.

Please refer to 5GMAX Massive MIMO Algorithm specification §5.1.2.2.3 (5G\_UP\_ALG\_700\_replaced([11101361](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knWcsvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) for more details.

The normalization per TRX-wise secures each TRX doesn't exceed maximum transmit power.

For the supported L1 HWs, refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_27410\_replaced([11100818](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knRkI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

## 2.3.5 Antenna Ports mapping (CPRI, eCPRI)

### 2.3.5.0-2 Antenna Ports mapping (CPRI, eCPRI) Overview for CPRI and eCPRI 7-2a (ID: 11100893)

**2.3.5.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11100905`)

Antenna Ports mapping (CPRI, eCPRI)
For 4 streams in baseband output (e.g. PDSCH, CSI-RS) previous sections (\*) indicate a mapping to (e)AxC(s) and polarization which must be followed in the same way for all channels.

CPRI has the constraint to only support the mapping below:

CPRI AxC mapping to polarization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | AxC | Polarization |
| stream 0 | 0 | +45 |
| stream 1 | 2 | +45 |
| stream 2 | 1 | -45 |
| stream 3 | 3 | -45 |

eCPRI has the flexibility to support the 2 mappings below:

eCPRI eAxC mapping to polarization : mappingA

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | eAxC\* | Polarization |
| stream 0 | 0 | +45 |
| stream 1 | 2 | +45 |
| stream 2 | 1 | -45 |
| stream 3 | 3 | -45 |

eCPRI eAxC mapping to polarization : mappingB

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | eAxC\* | Polarization |
| stream 0 | 0 | -45 |
| stream 1 | 2 | -45 |
| stream 2 | 1 | +45 |
| stream 3 | 3 | +45 |

\*eAxC indicate array index number for ceAxCId parameter in the messages on a 5G\_L2\_interface  e.g ceAxCId[eAxC]

From system performance point of view the 2 mappings supported by eCPRI are equivalent. Example of configuration at OAM side : mapping A is [128,0,129,1] and mapping B is [0,128,1,129].

Recommendation for sections (\*) reffering to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For general interworking simplicity, it is recommended  (as this is configurable at OAM) to use the mappingA for eCPRI in order to have same solution than CPRI.

CPRI radios served by the FHGW (Fronthaul Gateway) shall be configured with a mappingA (start from positive polarization).

(\*)Related requirements:

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_304\_replaced([11096941](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmzqGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_306\_replaced([11096987](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km0RJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_310\_replaced([11096995](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km0RKPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_427\_replaced([11097960](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km6-0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_627\_replaced([11100275](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMEnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_629\_replaced([11100312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_631\_replaced([11100338](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_633\_replaced([11100349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[CB010916-A] For the non-beamforming cells with 4x4 dlMimoMode configuration, when NRBTS.NRCELL.actAntportMap is set to TRUE, the DU shall perform the mapping of the 4 streams in baseband output (e.g. PDSCH, CSI-RS) to (e)AxC streams with the same way for CPRI and eCPRI regardless the polarizations. It means customer can freely configure the (e)AxC streams to the physical antenna ports. Refer to 5G\_UP\_1972\_replaced([11377324](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueVqtPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_1974\_replaced([11377358](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueVquPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_11261\_replaced([11377382](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueVqu_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_1978\_replaced([11377502](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueWRw_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for more details.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Baseband output | (e)AxC stream\* | Polarization | Physical ant |
| stream 0 | 0 | any\* | any |
| stream 1 | 2 | any\* | any |
| stream 2 | 1 | any\* | any |
| stream 3 | 3 | any\* | any |

\*: +45° or -45°

[End CB010916-A]

Note : CPRI/eCPRI support per board can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18\_replaced([11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 2.3.5.0-3 Antenna Ports mapping (CPRI, eCPRI) Overview for eCPRI 7-2e (ID: 11100910)

**2.3.5.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11100911`)

Antenna Ports mapping (CPRI, eCPRI)
For 4 internal streams in baseband output (e.g. PDSCH, CSI-RS) previous sections (\*) indicate a mapping polarization which must be followed in the same way for all channels.

Mapping between stream and polarization is done in Radio(L1) internally according to table below, and no eAxC links are involved.

eCPRI 7-2e internal stream mapping to polarization : mappingA

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Polarization |
| stream 0 | +45 |
| stream 1 | +45 |
| stream 2 | -45 |
| stream 3 | -45 |

Recommendation for sections (\*) reffering to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_689\_replaced([11100888](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSLOPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

(\*)Related requirements:

* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_304\_replaced([11096941](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmzqGvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_306\_replaced([11096987](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km0RJfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_310\_replaced([11096995](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km0RKPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_427\_replaced([11097960](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_km6-0_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))
* 5G\_UP\_ALG\_627\_replaced([11100275](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMEnPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_629\_replaced([11100312](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_631\_replaced([11100338](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrpPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G\_UP\_ALG\_633\_replaced([11100349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knMrp_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Note :eCPRI support per board can be found in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_18\_replaced([11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

## 2.3.6 RIM-RS Transmission

### 2.3.6.0-1 RIM-RS Transmission Document Owner Sebastien Bonnet (Nokia) (ID: 11100919)

### 2.3.6.1 Introduction

#### 2.3.6.1.0-1 Introduction Overview (ID: 11100924)

**2.3.6.1.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12682744`)

Introduction
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim is set to 3  or 4 , the DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B]and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall perform the RIM-RS signals generation(to 3GPP 38.211 V16.7 Clause 7.4.1.6.2) in the configured resource block groups and transmit them in the SFN and slot per Cell according to the configuration(see [11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Details are described in the following sub-chapters.

For eCPRI 7-2a, DU shall support RIM-RS transmission with the corresponding FCP (see [11378996](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueeNlfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))" .

For the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"), RIM-RS transmission shall support new corresponding FH mechanism.(see [11380671](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ueoloPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The last 2 DL OFDM symbols in special slot or DL slot will be used for RIM-RS transmission, other channels/signals will not be scheduled on these 2 symbols in the definite transmission slot (see [11360500](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucvIMvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

NOTE:1) RIM-RS cannot be configured on FR1-TDD 10MHz  and 15MHz , refer to ([11360676](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucwWUPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2) RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2

L1 High Level Processing Showing
Here is L1 High Level processing showing for eCPRI

Figure 1 RIM-RS L1 High Level Transmission Processing Reference Model for eCPRI

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to radio interface modules (RIM) and enhanced common public radio interface (eCPRI). The flowchart depicts the steps involved in processing signals, including sequence generation, resource mapping, IFFT (Inverse Fast Fourier Transform), CP (Cyclic Prefix) addition, FFT (Fast Fourier Transform), and CP removal.
The flowchart is divided into several sections. At the top, there's a block labeled "RIM Framework-1 Subcarrier offset New introduced" with arrows pointing to "Sequence generation" and "RIM-RS resource mapping".
To the left, a dashed red box encloses "DU(eCPRI 7-2a)/ Radio(eCPRI 7-2e) Function". An arrow from this box points to "Send to RU(eCPRI)".
The main processing path is within a larger dashed red box. It starts with "Sequence generation", which leads to "RIM-RS resource mapping". This is followed by "IFFT(4096)" and then "RIM-RS CP addition".
Another path, seemingly a reverse or complementary process, starts with "RIM eCPRI phase compensation", which leads to "RIM-RS resource de-mapping". This is followed by "FFT(4096)" and then "Normal CP removal".
Arrows connect these blocks, indicating the flow of data or control. There are also visual representations of data packets at different stages.
At the top, a data packet is shown as "CP I₀ (576) Symbol I₀ (4096) Symbol I₁ (4096)".
At the bottom left, another data packet is shown as "CP I₀ (288) Symbol I₀ (4096) CP I₁ (288) Symbol I₁ (4096)".
At the bottom right, after "Normal CP removal", a data packet is shown as "CP I₀ (576) Symbol I₀ (4096) Symbol I₁ (4096)", with a note "Different signals across 2 symbols after CP removal" pointing to the CP and symbol parts.
The overall diagram illustrates how signals are processed and transmitted within a RIM-eCPRI framework, highlighting the role of CP and resource mapping.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1495.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Figure1: RIM-RS - High Level transmission processing for eCPRI

Note: 1) This figure is based on 100MHz, SCS=30kHz.(FFT and IFFT samples shall be modified according to Bandwidth configuration)

2) To make RU transparent with the difference between normal CP (288 samples) with RIM-RS CP length(576 samples, see 5G\_UP\_IF\_21\_replaced([11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the block of Normal CP removal shall be implemented in DU. That is referred to "Normal CP removal" in Figure 1, RU in eCPRI can handle RIM-RS signal as normal CP length channel signal.

3) The function of RIM eCPRI phase compensation block please refer to 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12974\_replaced([11101247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

4) This figure is  just for general understanding, detail implementation please follow 5GMax algorithm section 3.9.

5) This High level chart is valid for both RIM Framework-0 (i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3) and RIM Framework-1 (i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim=4). Please note subcarrier offset function block is new introduced by RIM Framework-1, which can implement subcarrier level frequency mapping/de-mapping instead of PRB level frequency mapping/de-mapping to support mixed bandwidth transmission and detection.

6) The figure is stored in [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains blue text on a black background. The text appears to be a URL or a file path. The text reads: "RAN ARS - 5GX Spec. Repository - DLL-RI M- Tree\_View(sharepoint.com)". The text is underlined with a blue line.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1495.002.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

7) RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2

8) Subcarrier frequency offset is new introduced for the mapping of RIM-RS to the physical resources to support mixed bandwidths scenario in RIM Framework-1(see 5G\_UP\_11416\_replaced([11360126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucsr8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

9) Although there are different formulars between 5GMax algorithms and gNB-DU algorithms in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_12403\_replaced([11279177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUCrvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the determination of the physical resources that shall be used for RIM-RS transmission

, the same results are achieved.

10)[Before CB012564-C][CB012564-B]  the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, [Before CB009514-L]when NRBTS/actEcpri72e is set to "Ecpri72eULDL"[CB009514-L] when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"[End CB009514-L]

) shall support RIM Framework-0 [End CB012564-B][End CB012564-C]

**2.3.6.1.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11100972`)

L1 High Level Processing Showing
Here is L1 High Level processing showing for CPRI.

Figure 2 RIM-RS L1 High Level Transmission Processing Reference Model for CPRI

[image-0][image\_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to RIM-RS (Radio Interface Module - Reference Signal) within a DU (Distributed Unit) CPRI (Common Public Radio Interface) function. The process starts with "Sequence generation" which leads to "RIM-RS resource mapping". This is followed by "RIM-RS CPRI phase compensation" and then "IFFT (4096)".
There is a parallel path starting from "Sequence generation" that goes to "RIM CP addition". The output of "RIM CP addition" feeds into "RIM-RS CP handling". The output of "RIM-RS CP handling" is then combined with the output of "IFFT (4096)" and sent to "Send to RU". The "DU (CPRI) Function" is shown as a separate block that the main process feeds into.
Below the flowchart, there are representations of data structures. The first one shows "CP l₀ (576)" followed by "Symbol l₀ (4096)" and "Symbol l₁ (4096)". The second data structure, originating from "RIM-RS CP handling", shows "CP l₀ (288)", "Symbol l₀ (4096)", "CP l₁ (288)", and "Symbol l₁ (4096)".
An annotation at the top left indicates "RIM Framework-1 Subcarrier offset New introduced" with a yellow arrow pointing to "RIM-RS resource mapping". The entire process within the dashed red box is labeled as part of the "DU (CPRI) Function".[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-05280938.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Figure2: RIM-RS - High Level transmission processing for CPRI

Note: 1) This figure is  based on 100MHz, SCS=30kHz;(FFT and IFFT samples shall be modified according to Bandwidth configuration)

2) The function of RIM-RS CPRI phase compensation block please refer to [11101172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knUnffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ).

3) This figure is just for general understanding, detail implementation please follow 5GMax algorithm section 3.9.

4）This figure is implemented both for RIM framework-0 [CB010315-SR-C] and RIM framework-1 which new introduces subcarrier offset level in RIM-RS resource mapping [End CB010315-SR-C].

5) The figure source is in the URL:[RAN A&S- 5G-CB & 2/3/4G Spec. Repository of Architecture and Specification - Technical reference - All Documents](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/Forms/AllItems.aspx?id=%2Fsites%2FASCOP%2FAS%20Documents%2FFS1TAs%2DCFAMs%2FCB%2FCB010000%20%2D%20CB010499%2FCFAM%2FCB010315%20NR%20RIM%20feature%20support%20for%20FR1%2DTDD%20CPRI%2FTechnical%20reference&viewid=73435348%2D8379%2D4262%2Da3aa%2Dffe0b7322e0c)

---

### 2.3.6.2 RIM-RS resource allocation

#### 2.3.6.2.1 Overview

##### 2.3.6.2.1.0-1 Overview RIM-RS type 1 (ID: 11100981)

**2.3.6.2.1.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12646560`)

Overview
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim is set to 3  or 4 ,With the configuration of the locations of the corresponding RIM-RS REs and others 3GPP parameters like near-far, nSCID etc. (3GPP 38.211 V16.7 clause 7.4.1.6) (see [11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the DU with eCPRI 7-2a[CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B]and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall generate RIM-RS type 1 sequence, map it to time-domain resource and frequency-domain resource, and send it to the air.

---

##### 2.3.6.2.1.0-2 Overview RIM-RS type 2 (ID: 11101000)

**2.3.6.2.1.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12646673`)

Overview
When NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim is set to 4, With the configuration of the locations of the corresponding RIM-RS REs and others 3GPP parameters like near-far, nSCID etc. (3GPP 38.211 V16.7 clause 7.4.1.6) (see [11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), the DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-C] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-C] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE")  shall generate RIM-RS type 2 sequence, map it to time-domain resource and frequency-domain resource, and send it to the air.

---

The DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall generate RIM-RS signals (Specified in 3GPP 38.211 V16.7.0 clause 7.4.1.6) according to the configuration of

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains mathematical notation. The text reads: $n\_{\text{SCID}}$, $N\_{\text{RB}}^{\text{RIM}}$, $\bar{c}\_{\text{init}}(i)$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061937.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

specified in:

Ø ([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3 or 4 for RIM-RS Type1

Ø ([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4 for RIM-RS Type2

Note:

[image-1][image\_desc]The image contains a blurry mathematical expression. The expression appears to be $\bar{c}\_{init}(t)$.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061937.png[/image\_path][/image-1]

is one value, nothing to do with the index i.

RIM-RS time domain allocations
The DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B]] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall map RIM-RS signal to time-domain allocation according to the configuration of RIM-RS transmitted SFN , slot and the start symbols l0 timing information(specified in 3GPP 38.211 V16.70 clauses 7.4.1.6.2 and 7.4.1.6.4.3) specified in:

Ø  ([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3  or 4 ] for RIM-RS Type1

Ø  ([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4 for RIM-RS Type2

Below table gives the supported configurations for starting symbol index indicated by symbol index l0 within slot.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | L0 | comments |
| DL Slot | 12 | The last two symbols in DL slot |
| Special Slot | [2,4,6,8] | The last two DL symbols in Special slot |

Here is the possible RIM-RS transmission symbols.

[image-0][image\_desc]The image displays a visual representation of different slot configurations, likely related to telecommunications or data transmission. Each configuration is presented as a row of colored cells, with numbers or letters inside them, separated by vertical lines. The rows are labeled with descriptive text.
The first row, labeled "DL Slot", shows a sequence of blue cells numbered 0 through 11, followed by two red cells numbered 12 and 13.
The second row, labeled "Special Slot 10DL-2GP-2UL", starts with blue cells numbered 0 through 7. This is followed by two red cells numbered 8 and 9, and then two blue cells labeled "G" and two blue cells labeled "U".
The third row, labeled "Special Slot 8DL-4GP-2UL", begins with blue cells numbered 0 through 5. It then shows two red cells numbered 6 and 7, followed by four blue cells labeled "G" and two blue cells labeled "U".
The fourth row, labeled "Special Slot 6DL-4GP-4UL", starts with blue cells numbered 0 through 3. This is followed by two red cells numbered 4 and 5, and then four blue cells labeled "G" and four blue cells labeled "U".
The fifth row, labeled "Special Slot 4DL-6GP-4UL", begins with blue cells numbered 0 through 3. This is followed by six blue cells labeled "G" and four blue cells labeled "U".
The overall structure suggests a breakdown of time slots or resource allocation, where "DL" likely refers to Downlink, "GP" to Guard Period, and "UL" to Uplink. The colors (blue and red) and the labels (numbers, G, U) likely represent different types of data or control information within these slots.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/img-06061949.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

Note:8DL-4GP-2UL is not supported(see [11348206](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk0PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), From L1 POV, still keep it supported since the phase compensation looking up table is designed as support all.

---

#### 2.3.6.2.4 RIM-RS frequency domain allocations

##### 2.3.6.2.4.0-1 RIM-RS frequency domain allocations RIM-RS frequency domain allocations for RIM Framework-0 (ID: 11101094)

**2.3.6.2.4.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12646718`)

RIM-RS frequency domain allocations
The DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall map RIM-RS signal into the allocated resources where the Starting PRB index (specified in 3GPP 38.211 V16.7.0 clause 5.3.3)  and number of PRBs (  specified in 3GPP 38.211 V16.7.0 clause 7.4.1.6.4.3) are specified in:

Ø 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12735\_replaced([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3 for RIM-RS Type1

The DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall assume that the consecutive number of PRBs that are allocated for RIM-RS transmission (see above) does not exceed the cell bandwidth (see [11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Below table gives the supported configurations for the starting PRB index and number of PRBs (see [11360434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucuhJfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Possible value | Comments |
| the starting PRB index | [0,225] | from 20MHz to 100MHz |
| number of PRBs | 48,96 | from 20MHz to 100MHz |

Note: During CB006991-UPD001 discussion, one conclusion is drawn that there is no any restriction for the last PRB data fetching. and  5GMax simulation results show some PRBs IQ data can be blanked. Therefore the last PRB index shall be changed from 224 to 225.

Note: RIM-RS is not applicable for 20MHz TDD cells with PRB Blanking activated (actPRBBlanking is True) if number of blanked PRB is higher than 3 (nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL + nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL>3) as number of available PRBs is less than 48 (see[11360676](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucwWUPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 2.3.6.2.4.0-2 RIM-RS frequency domain allocations RIM-RS frequency domain allocations for RIM Framework-1 (ID: 11101114)

**2.3.6.2.4.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12646725`)

RIM-RS frequency domain allocations
The DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-C] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-C] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE")shall map RIM-RS signal into the allocated resources which are not necessary aligned with the 1st subcarrier of the starting PRB index(see [11360126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucsr8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). DU  with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-C] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-C] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall use the following parameters to determine the physical resources for RIM-RS transmission: start PRB index, sub-carrier offset and PRB number that are specified in:

Ø  ([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4 for RIM-RS Type1 and RIM-RS Type2

DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-C] and CPRI [End CB010315-SR-C] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall assume that the consecutive number of PRBs that are allocated for RIM-RS transmission (see above) does not exceed the cell bandwidth (see [11094261](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmaoifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Below table gives the supported configurations for start PRB index, sub-carrier offset, and PRB number (see [11360434](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucuhJfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | possible value | Comments |
| start PRB index | [0,...,225] | from 20MHz to 100MHz |
| PRB number | 48,96 | from 20MHz to 100MHz |
| sub-carrier offset | [0,...,11] | from 20MHz to 100MHz |

Note: RIM-RS is not applicable for 20MHz TDD cells with PRB Blanking activated (actPRBBlanking is True) if number of blanked PRB is higher than 3 (nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeDL + nbrOfBlankedPRBsHighEdgeDL>3) as number of available PRBs is less than 48 (see [11360676](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucwWUPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note:

1) To support Mixed bandwidths scenario in RIM Framework-1, subcarrier level mapping/de-mapping is introduced (see [11100939](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSySPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ),[11117993](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqAIB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and [11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5GMax algorithm support (see chapter 3.9 in 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification)

2) when RIM Framework-1 is enable(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) and  subcarrier offset is not equal to 0, PRB number is absolute value to indicate the RE mapping shall be (48*12)/(96*12), the actual occupied PRBs will be (48+1)/(96+1), this actual occupied PRBs leaves to be implemented by EFS. (see [11360126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucsr8vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

### 2.3.6.3 RIM-RS power setting

#### 2.3.6.3.0-1 RIM-RS power setting (ID: 11101127)

**2.3.6.3.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12646730`)

RIM-RS power setting
Input:

rimrsTransmitPower computed per RIM-RS resource (see [11118655](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqEZefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and indicates to DU (see [11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) both for RS1 and RS2)

Algorithm:

DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall determine the DL RIM-RS EPRE according to the value of rimrsTransmitPower

Output:

Power setting per RE for the RIM-RS signal both for DU with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B]and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE")

---

### 2.3.6.4 RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping

#### 2.3.6.4.0-1 RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping Overview (ID: 11101142)

**2.3.6.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11101151`)

RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping
Since RIM-RS is not received by UE, there is no antenna port number defined in 3GPP 38.211 V16.7.0 clause 7.4.1.6.3. the DU [CB012564-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, [Before CB009514-L]when NRBTS/actEcpri72e is set to "Ecpri72eULDL"[CB009514-L] when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"[End CB009514-L]

) [End CB012564-B]  shall ignore then antenna port number since it's valueless. but shall consider the RIM-RS beam pattern ID and it shall implement the following functions:

 RIM-RS transmission scheme for 2x2 DL MIMO mode and beamforming, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13003\_replaced([11101155](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knUnd_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 RIM-RS transmission scheme for Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13006\_replaced([11101164](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knUnevvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The RIM-RS beam pattern ID is specified in:

 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12735\_replaced([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3 or 4 for RIM-RS Type1

 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12735\_replaced([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4 for RIM-RS Type2

[CB012564-B]

Note:

For eCPRI 7-2e, Non-beamforming is not in scope in Radio(L1).

[End CB012564-B]

---

#### 2.3.6.4.0-2 RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping RIM-RS Non-beamforming with 2x2 DL MIMO mode and beamforming (ID: 11101155)

**2.3.6.4.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12763124`)

RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping
For RIM-RS transmission, the mapping described here shall be applied for the below gNB configurations (similar as for CSI-RS for tracking):

In the DU

* Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = TRUE) regardless the DL MIMO mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))
* Non beamforming configuration for 2x2 DL MIMO (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = 2x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing or 2x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing)

In the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"):

* Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = TRUE) regardless the DL MIMO mode (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The mapping for RIM-RS signals to polarizations shall be done as given in below table.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Signal** | **Precoding vector** | **eAxC [CB010315-SR-B**]/AxC[End CB010315-SR-B]\*\*\*\* | **Polarization** |
| RIM-RS | [1 1] | 0 | +45 of X-Pol1 |
|  |  | 1 | -45 of X-Pol1 |

Note:

For eCPRI 7-2e, Non-beamforming is not in scope in Radio(L1).

---

#### 2.3.6.4.0-3 RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping RIM-RS Non-beamforming with 4x4 DL MIMO mode (ID: 11101164)

**2.3.6.4.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `12763131`)

RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping
For RIM-RS transmission, the mapping described here shall be applied for the below gNB configurations (same as for CSI-RS for tracking):

* Non Beamforming configuration (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actBeamforming = FALSE) and 4x4 or 4x2 DL MIMO (i.e. NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/dlMimoMode = '4x4 or 4x2 Closed Loop Spatial Multiplexing' or '4x4 or 4x2 Open Loop Spatial Multiplexing' (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_29\_replaced([11094546](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmdr1vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

The mapping for RIM-RS signals to polarizations shall be done as given in below table.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Signal** | **Precoding vector** | **eAxC [CB010315-**SR-B**]/AxC[End CB010315-**SR-B**]*****\****\* | **Polarization** |
| RIM-RS | [1 1 1 1] | 0 | +45 of X-Pol1 |
|  |  | 1 | -45 of X-Pol1 |
|  |  | 2 | +45 of X-Pol2 |
|  |  | 3 | -45 of X-Pol2 |

---

### 2.3.6.5 RIM-RS phase compensation

#### 2.3.6.5.0-1 RIM-RS phase compensation RIM-RS phase compensation overview for eCPRI (ID: 11101172)

**2.3.6.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11101182`)

RIM-RS phase compensation
In DU with eCPRI 7-2a:

To make RU transparent for RIM-RS transmission, some specific RIM-RS phase compensation shall be designed (see ~~5G\_UP\_ALG\_13029\_replaced([11100967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSyTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),~~ 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13030\_replaced([11100943](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSySfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

It will be specified in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12974\_replaced([11101247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

**[CB012564-B]**

In the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, **[Before CB009514-L]when NRBTS/actEcpri72e is set to "Ecpri72eULDL"**[CB009514-L] when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"**[End CB009514-L]**\*\*\*\*

) :

The same RIM RS processing mechanism will be kept, that is the specific RIM-RS phase compensation shall be inhertied to keep legacy RIM RS processing chain in the Radio, then there is  no needs to introduce new RIM RS processing channel in the Radio  (see ~~5G\_UP\_ALG\_13029\_replaced([11100967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSyTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),~~ 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13030\_replaced([11100943](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSySfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12974\_replaced([11101247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))  )

**[End CB012564-B]**

Note:RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2

---

#### 2.3.6.5.0-2 RIM-RS phase compensation RIM-RS phase compensation overview for CPRI (ID: 12655624)

**2.3.6.5.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `12655629`)

RIM-RS phase compensation
DU with CPRI shall perform RIM-RS CPRI phase compensation function in RIM-RS Transmitter processing (depicted in [11100967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSyTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and it shall meet the requirement of  introduced by RIM-RS signal itself.

The DU shall compute and store one static look up table according to configuration during cell set up.(see ([11274097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpFZJPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Detail algorithm please refer to 5GMax Algorithm section 3.9.

[CB010315-SR-C]Note:RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2[End CB010315-SR-C]

---

### 2.3.6.6 Supported RIM-RS Transmission

#### 2.3.6.6.0-1 Supported RIM-RS Transmission Supported RIM-RS transmission (ID: 11101188)

**2.3.6.6.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `12646752`)

Supported RIM-RS Transmission
For TDD FR1,when NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 3  or 4 , Loki and Thor DU HW with eCPRI 7-2a [CB010315-SR-B] and CPRI [Before CB010315-SR-C](only with actNrRim equals to 3) [End CB010315-SR-C][End CB010315-SR-B] and the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE") shall support the below algorithms for RIM-RS transmission:

Ø  RIM-RS transmission function block, see ([11100921](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSyQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Ø  RIM-RS resource allocation, see ([11100975](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knTZUPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Ø  RIM-RS power setting, see ([11101123](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knUAbPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Ø  RIM-RS transmission scheme and antenna mapping, see ([11101140](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knUncvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Ø  RIM-RS phase compensation, see ([11101170](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knUnfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Ø  RIM-RS 5GMax algorithm, see 5GMax-5G New Radio Algorithm Specification V11.173 section 3.9.

Note: RIM-RS in RIM Framework-1(i.e. NRBTS/NRCELL/actNrRim = 4) shall include both RS type 1 and type 2

---

## 2.3.7 OFDM signal generation and symbol phase compensation

### 2.3.7.0-2 OFDM signal generation and symbol phase compensation OFDM signal baseband generation (ID: 11101211)

**2.3.7.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `11101224`)

OFDM signal generation and symbol phase compensation
The OFDM baseband signal generation is specified in 38.211 section 5.3 for all the DL PBCH/PDCCH/PDSCH/CSI-RS **and RIM-RS channels/signals.**

For CPRI, L1 shall generate the OFDM baseband signal while satisfying the AxC sampling rate specified for TDD FR1 and FDD in 5G\_UP\_2478\_replaced([11389682](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufPCoPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_2481\_replaced([11389715](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufPpofvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) respectively. The sampling rate depends on the cell bandwidth size and the numerology/subcarrier spacing.

For eCPRI/eCRPI 7-2e, OFDM signal baseband generation shall not be performed in DU(L1) / Radio(L1). It is done on RU side. Please refer to 5G\_RAN\_RF\_1037\_replaced([11288323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tgrn4PvYEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For RIM-RS OFDM signal generation, additional operation shall be performed in DU **[CB012564-B] or** In the Radio (for eCPRI 7-2e, **[Before CB009514-L]when NRBTS/actEcpri72e is set to "Ecpri72eULDL"**[CB009514-L] when NRCELL/actEcpri72e is set to "TRUE"**[End CB009514-L]*****\****\*

)**[End CB012564-B]  for 3GPP compliancy, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13030\_replaced([11100943](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSySfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Note: For CPRI, the IFFT size for the OFDM signal generation is specified in L1 EFS in [11425878](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-yLdPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) and the OFDM signal generation in 5G\_L1\_3728\_replaced([11427567](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-6HQ_vvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The DL OFDM signal generation is in L1HW supported as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17675\_replaced([11101274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

11101244:
OFDM signal generation and symbol phase compensation
11101244:
OFDM signal generation and symbol phase compensation
Unlike LTE, Dc (Direct carrier) subcarrier for 5G/NR is used to send the signal as per the formula in 38.211 section 5.4.

As Dc subcarriers for the transmitter and the receiver are not necessarily perfectly synchronized,

as in initial access UE does not know the DL system bandwidth center, i.e. DL system bandwidth center may not be the same as the SSB bandwidth center,

a mismatch between UE’s baseband center frequency assumption and the actual signal center frequency is unavoidable.

This would result in unknown phase rotation across OFDM symbols and UE performance impact until frequency mismatch computed and compensation applied.

To cope with this issue, 3GPP introduced phase compensation operation and included it in the up conversion formula in 38.211 section 5.4.

It corresponds to the following term which is symbol basis and it depends on the numerology and on the configured carrier frequency (arfcn).

[image-0][image\_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is:
$e^{-j2\pi f\_0(t\_{start,l}^{\mu} + N\_{CP,l}^{\mu}T\_c)}$[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1562.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0]

where the different variables are defined in 38.211 section 5.3.1.

For CPRI, L1 shall apply the phase compensation for each OFDM symbol for all the DL channels and signals.

For FR2, this phase compensation term is not the same between SSB and other DL signals/channels as they are configured with different numerologies, see 5G\_UP\_IF\_21\_replaced([11491448](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tRt23vv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The phase compensation term is computed according to the algorithm in 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_1997\_replaced([11274097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpFZJPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

For eCPRI[CB009170-D]/eCRPI 7-2e[End CB009170-D], OFDM symbol phase compensation shall not be performed in DU(L1)[CB009170-D]/Radio(L1)[End CB009170-D]. It is done on RU side. Please refer to 5G\_RAN\_RF\_1037\_replaced([11288323](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_Tgrn4PvYEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For RIM-RS symbol phase compensation, additional operation shall be performed in DU(L1) for 3GPP compliancy, see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12972\_replaced([11101172](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knUnffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G\_UP\_ALG\_12974\_replaced([11101247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: More details about the implementation status could be found in 5GMAX algo spec. section 4.1.3 and in 5G\_L1\_3729\_replaced([11427668](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_e-6HSPvvEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The DL OFDM symbol phase compensation is in L1HW supported as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17675\_replaced([11101274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB009170-D]

Note2:

- RIM-RS is not supported in eCPRI 7-2e.

- "5G-L1-DL" is changed to "DU(L1)" since this version as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_20317\_replaced([11094116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmZaYfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). (Note kept only in this version)

[End CB009170-D]

For eCPRI FH:

the function of RIM-RS phase compensation in RIM-RS Transmitter processing (depicted in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13030\_replaced([11100943](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSySfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall meet the two conflicting requirements:

* One is  [image-0][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $N\_{cp}^{RIM}T\_c$. The expression is written in a light gray color against a white background. The letters and symbols are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1566.001.png[/image\_path][/image-0] which introduced by RIM-RS signal itself,
* One is to perform the operation represented by  [image-1][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $N\_{cp}^{RIM}T\_c$. The expression is written in a light gray color against a white background. The letters and symbols are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1566.001.png[/image\_path][/image-1] term in specific RIM-RS signal expression (as per 38.211 section 5.4)
* The other one is to exclude the impacts of normal PDSCH phase compensation introduced by RU in eCPRI.
* The second one is not to introduce in RU additional signal processing impacts for the symbol phase compensation, kept unchanged and common for all DL channels/signals in RU eCPRI (see 5G\_UP\_ALG\_16310\_replaced([11101225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knVOjvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) .

For this purpose, the 5G-L1-DL shall compute and store one static look up table according to configuration during cell set up (see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_1997\_replaced([11274097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpFZJPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). For L1 algorithm details please refer to 5GMax Algorithm section 3.9.

[CB010315] [DRAFT]

For CPRI FH:

The function of RIM-RS CPRI phase compensation in RIM-RS Transmitter processing (depicted in 5G\_UP\_ALG\_13029\_replaced([11100967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knSyTfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) shall meet the requirement of [image-2][image\_desc]The image contains the mathematical expression $N\_{cp}^{RIM}T\_c$. The expression is written in a light gray color against a white background. The letters and symbols are slightly blurred.[/image\_desc][image\_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng\_files/images/HTML\_m22dng\_5G\_UP\_Algorithms\_file\_1566.002.png[/image\_path][/image-2] introduced by RIM-RS signal itself.

The 5G-L1-DL shall compute and store one static look up table according to configuration during cell set up.(see 5G\_CP\_ARC\_ALG\_1997\_replaced([11274097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpFZJPvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) Detail algorithm please refer to 5GMax Algorithm section 3.9.[End CB010315]

The RIM-RS phase compensation in RIM-RS Transmitter is in L1HW supported as per 5G\_UP\_ALG\_17675\_replaced([11101274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1lvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

### 2.3.7.0-5 OFDM signal generation and symbol phase compensation Supported DL OFDM signal baseband generation in L1 HW (ID: 11101274)

**2.3.7.0-5.0-7**  (ID: `13344017`)

OFDM signal generation and symbol phase compensation
The DL OFDM baseband signal generation is supported in L1HW as follows:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **L1 HW** | **Loner** | **Loki** | **Thor** | **Thor** **(Radio 7-2e)** | **GPU** | **First Feature** |
| OFDM symbol generation & phase compensation ([11101211](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knVOifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), ([11101225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knVOjvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) | Supported | Supported | Supported | Supported | **[CB016066-C]  Supported**[End CB016066-C]\*\*\*\* |  |
| RIM-RS OFDM symbol generation ([11101211](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knVOifvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | - | Supported | Supported | Supported | - | CB006991-A |
| RIM-RS symbol phase compensation ([11101247](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_knV1kvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) | - | Supported | Supported | Supported | - | CB006991-A |
